<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Earthen Destrier](/Monsters.aspx?ID=651)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

This wave of dirt takes the crude likeness of the melded forequarters of a charging warhorse and a rocky knight wielding a rudimentary lance of gray stone.

</column>

hoof +14,

**Earth Glide**   An earthen destrier can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, it moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

**Lancing Charge**   If the destrier moved at least 10 feet directly before its lance arm Strike, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its damage roll.

**Tilting Strike** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Trigger** The earthen destrier tramples a creature; **Effect** The earthen destrier makes a lance arm Strike against the creature it's trampling at a –5 penalty.

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Medium or smaller, hoof, DC 20

</column>

markdown:
Few beasts have inspired as many legends, poems, songs, and myths in as many cultures, as this bizarre draconic creature. The jabberwock hails from the fey realm of the First World and is part of a group of powerful [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) creatures known collectively as the Tane. Like all members of the Tane, jabberwocks are living creatures that age, eat, drink, and sleep, but do not reproduce normally. Instead, they are created directly by the godlike Eldest who rule the First World, and they serve at the pleasure (or frustration) of these capricious and mysterious entities. Believed to be the most powerful of all the Tane, jabberwocks are typically only ever created with one purpose: to wreak destruction and havoc upon the Material Plane. One of the Eldest might send a jabberwock to devastate a country, continent, or even entire world in order to get revenge for some slight made against them by a single mortal. Sometimes, no specific insult or injury is required, as some of the Eldest resent the very existence of the Material Plane and periodically create jabberwocks simply to express the wrath of the First World against its younger and less chaotic sibling. Once a jabberwock arrives on the Material Plane, its first order of business is to seek out a lair. It prefers dwelling in remote forest locations about a day's flight from civilization—the more dangerous the woodland is to those who might eventually come hunting it, the better! The relationship between jabberwocks and [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=308) weapons is the subject of much debate and speculation among scholars, and the various poems, songs, and legends about the jabberwock do little to clarify the issue, conflicting and diverging on this point. Some believe that _vorpal _weapons were first created specifically to combat jabberwocks, but others take the story one step further. They cite evidence in certain ancient myths that there may once have been only a single, unique jabberwock, so powerful that nothing could so much as scratch it—nothing, that is, except for the first _vorpal _sword, crafted for that very purpose. So epic was the resulting battle that it created strange echoes throughout reality, and as a result, these echoes, in the form of _vorpal _weapons, can now be found on many worlds. Those who subscribe to this belief claim that the jabberwocks seen today are but pale imitations of this proto-jabberwock, and they speculate that only the combined efforts of several Eldest would be sufficient to create such a creature again. Variant JabberwocksThe Eldest have sometimes altered certain aspects of the jabberwocks they create. Some of the more notorious or legendary variant jabberwocks include the following. **Frumious Jabberwock**: Frumious jabberwocks have two heads. They are level 24 and gain an extra action on each of their turns they can use only to make a jaws Strike. **Mimsy Jabberwock**: Typically hailing from wintry regions of the First World, mimsy jabberwocks have glowing blue-white scales, resist cold instead of fire, and have eyes that burn with blue flames that deal cold damage instead of fire damage. **Slithy Jabberwock**: Wreaking destruction beneath the waves, these sinuous and slimy jabberwocks are amphibious, have a swim Speed of 80 feet instead of the normal fly Speed, and do not have a wing Strike. A slithy jabberwock's whiffling aura is activated whenever it swims, makes a tail Strike, or Burbles. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 51 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 [Jabberwock](/Monsters.aspx?ID=652) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 150 **Perception** +40; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent 120 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +40, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +44, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +41, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +38, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +40 **Str** +11 **Dex** +7 **Con** +10 **Int** +4 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +8 **Planar Acclimation** The jabberwock always treats the plane it is currently located on as its home plane. --- **AC** 49 **Fort** +39 **Ref** +37 **Will** +40 **HP** 500 , regeneration 25 (deactivated by [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=308) weapons) **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** fire 20 **Weaknesses** _vorpal_ weapons 20, vorpal fear** **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 100 feet, DC 43
**Vorpal Fear** A jabberwock damaged by a vorpal weapon becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (or frightened 4 on a critical hit).
**Claws That Catch** **Trigger** A creature within the jabberwock's reach uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action, leaves a square during a move action, makes a ranged attack, or uses a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) action; **Effect** The jabberwock makes a claw Strike against the triggering creature. If the Strike hits, the jabberwock disrupts the triggering action. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** jaws +42 ([deadly 2d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+19 piercing **Melee** claw +42 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+19 slashing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tail +42 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+19 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Melee** wing +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+19 bludgeoning **Ranged** eyes of flame +42 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 10d6 fire plus 4d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 43 - **Constant (10th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Burble** The jabberwock creates a blast of strange noises and shouted nonsense in the various languages it knows (and invariably some languages it doesn't know), creating one of two effects. The jabberwock can't Burble again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Confusion** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 60 feet. Each creature in the emanation must succeed at a DC 46 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Sonic Beam** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The jabberwock focuses its Burbling into a 60.foot line of sonic energy that deals 24d6 sonic damage to creatures in the area (DC 46 basic Reflex save).
**Whiffling** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) **Trigger** The jabberwock Flies or makes a wing Strike; **Effect** The jabberwock's wings whiffle, creating [severe winds](/Rules.aspx?ID=643) within a 30-foot emanation. These winds move outward from the jabberwock, and they persist until the start of the jabberwock's next turn. During this time, flight of any kind in the emanation requires a successful DC 43 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30), and creatures flying toward the jabberwock are moving through greater difficult terrain. Creatures on the ground in the emanation must succeed at a DC 43 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to approach the jabberwock. **Jaws That Bite** If the jabberwock makes a jaws attack and rolls a natural 19 on the d20 roll, the attack is a critical hit. This has no effect if the 19 would be a failure.
Jabberwock Treasure Jabberwocks have no interest in treasure for its own sake, but they gather objects of great value or power specifically to entice mortals into seeking it out. As a result, jabberwocks quickly amass impressive hoards of treasures taken from nearby areas.
Jabberwocks and Dragons Although jabberwocks may fear [ <%RULES%371%%> vorpal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=308) <%END> weapons and are filled with rage against all mortal creatures, few things invite a jabberwock's wrath more than a [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) dragon. Some scholars believe that in the same way the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) is believed to have been a template or “first draft” of the Material Plane, dragons were created in the likeness of jabberwocks. A jabberwock that learns of a nearby dragon will attempt to kill it to the exclusion of all other priorities.
name: Jabberwock

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sand Sentry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=653)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Shifting back and forth between a detailed likeness of a human and a muted and featureless bipedal form, this creature of pure sand moves with an eerie grace. The sand sentry is often called upon by spellcasters to stand guard over an area of great importance—these elementals are patient participants in such roles, making them well suited for long-term service.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Sand Sentry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=653)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 109

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40)

**Languages**
[Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 94

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Glass Armor** When the sand sentry takes fire or lightning damage, its outer layer of sand fuses into sheets of hardened glass for 1 minute. This increases the sand sentry's AC to 26 and grants it resistance 5 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, piercing, and slashing damage. A sand sentry can't use earth glide while glass armor is active.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 50 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +17,
**Damage** 2d8+8 bludgeoning plus blinding sand

**Earth Glide**   A sand sentry can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, it moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

**Blinding Sand**   When the sand sentry critically hits with a fist Strike, the target is blinded for 1 round.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Sand Mimics</title>

Sand sentries have no true culture or society of their own, but they are endlessly fascinated with the society and culture of humanoids they encounter. They can shape their appearance to mimic any similarly sized humanoids, and although they always remain obviously composed of sand, they do their best to mimic the day-to-day activities they observe other humanoids performing. Of course, structures and objects sand sentries build from sand never last, but this never seems to stifle their obsession.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-42" />
name: Sand Sentry

This squat and stony creature looks like an armadillo with a large raised back and a flail-like tail. The granite glyptodont has no true need to feed, as with all elementals, yet it does seem to linger after transforming the flesh of those it calcifies, as if the mere proximity of flesh fossilizing into stone pleases it somehow. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Granite Glyptodont](/Monsters.aspx?ID=654) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 109 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense (imprecise) 90 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 145 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet; earth glide **Melee** tail +20 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile piercing](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d12+9 bludgeoning plus calcification **Calcification** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) A blow from a granite glyptodont's tail hardens the flesh of the creature struck. The target must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude saving throw or become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (or slowed 2 on a critical failure). Further failed saves against calcification increase the value of the slowed condition. Once a creature's actions are reduced to 0 by calcification, that creature becomes [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30). If the creature isn't petrified, the slowed conditions end once 1 minute passes without the creature failing a save against calcification.
Every 24 hours after it was petrified, the creature can attempt a DC 26 Fortitude save to recover. On a success, it becomes flesh again, but is slowed 1 for the next 24 hours. On a critical success, the creature recovers and isn't slowed. On a failure, the creature remains petrified, but can try again in 24 hours. On a critical failure, the petrification is permanent, and the creature can't attempt any more saves. **Earth Glide** A granite glyptodont can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, it moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.
name: Granite Glyptodont

These creatures look like roiling clouds of black smoke churning above a mound of burning refuse. Leering faces form and fade in the smoke above, while the burning trash writhes in spasmodic lurches, obviously alive. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Filth Fire](/Monsters.aspx?ID=656) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 110 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision **Languages** [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18); can't speak any language **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9 **Str** +1 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Smoke Vision** The filth fire ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +13 **Will** +9 **HP** 70 **Immunities** bleed, fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** cold 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** burning lash +13 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+3 bludgeoning plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** ember ball +13 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+3 bludgeoning plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Noxious Burst** Toxic materials and churning rubbish within the filth fire's body explode in one of three ways. The filth fire chooses the effect, but it can't make the same choice twice in a row.
  • Fiery Beam ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The filth fire expels a 30-foot line of flame that deals 3d6 fire damage with a DC 21 basic Reflex save.
  • Shrapnel Blast ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The filth fire shoots jagged rubbish out in a 5-foot emanation that deals 2d12 piercing damage with a DC 21 basic Reflex save.
  • Toxic Fumes ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The filth fire belches a 15-foot cone of toxic smoke that deals 2d6 poison damage (DC 21 basic Fortitude save). A creature that fails is also [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (or sickened 2 on a critical failure).
name: Filth Fire

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Striding Fire](/Monsters.aspx?ID=657)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An embodiment of the speed and chaos of a spreading blaze, a striding fire appears as a lithe and long-limbed humanoid composed of shifting-hued flames churning within a skeleton-like framework.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Striding Fire](/Monsters.aspx?ID=657)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 111

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision

**Languages**
[Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Smoke Vision** The striding fire ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 115

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Weaknesses**
cold 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d8+5 bludgeoning plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Burning Rush** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The striding fire Strides up to double its Speed in a straight line. Its movement during this Stride doesn't trigger reactions. Any creature the striding fire was adjacent to at any point during this Stride must attempt a DC 24 basic Reflex save. If it critically fails, it is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) by a wave of heated air. The striding fire can't use Burning Rush for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-43" />
name: Striding Fire

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Magma Scorpion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=658)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Whether skittering through Abyssal wastelands or basking in the searing sand of the deepest deserts, magma scorpions have charred carapaces constantly emitting vision-warping waves of heat.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ElementalFire_MagmaScorpion.png" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>

**Dex** +3

**Con** +5

**Int** -4

**Cha** +0
</row>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 28 

**Fort** +19 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +16

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 155

bleed, fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

tail sting +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),

**Damage** 1d6+9 fire plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Magma Scorpion Venom**  ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 fire damage (1 round) and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13); **Stage 2** 3d6 fire damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dust Mephit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=659)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Whining and self-important creatures, dust mephits are known for their tendency to pronounce doom and gloom at every opportunity, lamenting every insignificant and minor difficulty as though it were an insurmountable obstacle and making themselves out to be heroes for struggling valiantly against their cruel fates. They tend to be thin and gaunt, with dusky brown and gray skin and wings.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ElementalMephit_DustMephit.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Dust Mephit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=659)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 112

**Perception** +3; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** -2

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -1
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 16
([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 2 (in dust or sand))

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 slashing

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 17
- **2nd**
[Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136)

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) The dust mephit breathes a cloud of dust in a 15-foot cone that deals 2d6 slashing damage to each creature within the area (DC 17 basic Reflex save). The dust mephit can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ice Mephit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=660)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Though capable of the same depths of cruelty as their fire mephit kin, ice mephits carry theirs out in a more calculating way. Unlike most mephits, these pale blue mephits prefer solitude to company. Lacking in empathy, they are known for their callousness, and have a reputation for brutal honesty. They also have few moral qualms about acting selfishly and exploiting weakness for their own gain, should the chance arise.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 112

**Perception** +3; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** -2

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 18
([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 2 (when touching ice or snow))

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, cold, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Weaknesses**
fire 3

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4 slashing and 1d4 cold

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 17, attack +9
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245)

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) The ice mephit breathes shards of ice in a 15-foot cone that deals 1d6 cold damage and 1d6 piercing damage to each creature within the area (DC 17 basic Reflex save). The ice mephit can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ooze Mephit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=661)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Slick in every sense of the word, ooze mephits are unapologetic sycophants, shamelessly flattering anyone they may be able to con a favor out of, although their lack of intelligence and subtlety means that only the most vain or trusting targets fail to see through their honeyed words. Lacking proper bones, these ocher-colored mephits have bodies consisting primarily of ooze and muck. They smell disgusting, and their touch leaves stains that are almost impossible to get out.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

</column>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +8,
**Damage** 1d6+3 bludgeoning

[Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140)

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The ooze mephit spits toxic slime in a 15-foot cone that deals 2d6 poison damage to each creature within the area (DC 17 basic Reflex save). The ooze mephit can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Steam Mephit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=662)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These brash and arrogant creatures are among the more energetic mephits. They are quick to nominate themselves the leaders of any group, barking out orders in shrill, high-pitched voices and insisting on having everything done their way. Even when they aren't bossing others around, the constant hiss of steam escaping from their pores ensures that there is never a quiet moment.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

**Fort** +9

([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 2 (in boiling water or steam))

[Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210)

**Boiling Rain** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The steam mephit calls down a rain boiling water that surrounds them in a 10-foot emanation. Creatures in the area take 2d8 fire damage (DC 17 basic Reflex save), and small unattended flames (such as torches) are extinguished.

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The steam mephit spits a cloud of steam in a 15-foot cone that deals 2d6 fire damage to each creature within the area (DC 17 basic Reflex save). The steam mephit can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

The tentacled mist stalker shrouds itself in a cloak of mist through which its single, never-blinking eye can see with clarity, allowing it an advantage when stalking its prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Mist Stalker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=664) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 114 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), mist vision **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Mist Cloud** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) 15 feet. The mist stalker is surrounded by mist. Creatures in the aura are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4). If wind disperses the aura, it returns automatically at the start of the mist stalker's turn. This cloud is suppressed in water.
**Mist Vision** The mist stalker ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from mist and fog. --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +12 **Will** +11 **HP** 58 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) --- **Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** tentacle +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+4 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d8+4 bludgeoning, DC 21 **Solidify Mist** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) The mist stalker makes its mist cloud congeal, causing the aura to be difficult terrain until the start of the mist stalker's next turn. In addition, the mist stalker can make the mist even thicker around a single Medium or smaller creature within the cloud. The creature must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or it is no longer in the mist cloud's emanation.
name: Mist Stalker

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Blizzardborn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=665)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Freezing and thawing in a near-constant loop, blizzardborn resemble humanoid forms composed of a mixture of partially melted snow and sleet. These elementals move with crunching strides, their bodies constantly sloshing and sloughing off shards of ice. Because of their ability to refreeze, blizzardborn can travel into warmer environments safely, though they tend to look more like slush in these areas.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 115

[Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Snow Vision** The blizzardborn ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from falling snow.

**Will** +14

**HP** 105

bleed, cold, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Weaknesses**
fire 5

**Shattering Ice** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An enemy hits the blizzardborn with an attack that deals physical damage; **Effect** A portion of the blizzardborn's body shatters into an explosion of razor sharp ice crystals and blinding snow that deals 2d6 piercing damage to opponents in a 5-foot emanation (DC 24 basic Reflex save). Anyone who fails is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round (or 3 rounds on a critical failure).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, ice burrow 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ice claw +17 ([versatile bludgeoning](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 2d6+8 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent cold](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Ice Burrow**   The blizzardborn can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through ice or snow with a Speed of 20 feet. It moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Icewyrm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=666)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Resembling wingless, serpentine dragons formed of jagged ice and shot through with veins of nearly frozen water, these elementals dwell within icebergs and enjoy striking out at passing ships or creatures. They're especially common in frigid stretches of ocean in the Plane of Water, where icebergs cluster together into enormous islands of ice.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/ElementalWater_Icewyrm.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Icewyrm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=666)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=207" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=57" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 115

**Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +7

**Con** +5

**Int** -1

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 30 

**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +17 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 185

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, cold, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)

**Weaknesses**
fire 10

**Explosion** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) When the icewyrm dies, it explodes, dealing 8d6 cold damage to each creature in a 10-foot emanation (DC 27 basic Reflex save).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, ice burrow 20 feet, swim 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +23 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d12+13 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d6+13 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent cold](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ice shard +23 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6+13 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent cold](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The icewyrm breathes a 60-foot line of freezing shards of razor-sharp ice, dealing 3d12 cold damage and 3d12 piercing damage to every creature in the line (DC 29 basic Reflex save). The icewyrm can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

**Ice Burrow**   The Icewyrm can Burrow through ice or snow with a Speed of 20 feet. It moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Elemental Wyrms</title>

The icewyrm is the most widely encountered elemental wyrm, but others exist as well, including the blazing firewyrm, the sleek but somewhat smaller sparkwyrm, and the largest of them all, the immense and acidic sludgewyrm.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-45" />

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mastodon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=668)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Not to be confused with the larger mammoth, mastodons are primeval elephants who dwell predominantly in temperate forests. There, they travel in close-knit social groups and feed on the forest vegetation.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Elephant_Mastodon.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Mastodon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=668)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=9" />

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Fetchling Scout](/Monsters.aspx?ID=669)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The people known today as fetchlings are a distinct ancestry descended from generations of humans who became trapped ages ago on the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11). No longer human at all, these people, who call themselves kayals, have become monochromatic in coloration, with flesh tones and hair colors varying between white, black, and all shades of gray. Their limbs are lithe and willowy, and their eyes are generally solid yellow, yellow-green, or white, though a rare few have a purple or blue glow.

 Fetchlings have developed their own complex societies in the Shadow Plane, often under the tolerance of or in servitude to the strange, malignant creatures there, such as the sinister velstracs or the enigmatic d'ziriaks. While individual fetchlings can be any alignment, they are all survivors in a harsh environment, which leads to a tendency toward pragmatism. Fetchling clothing mimics the regions they dwell in, with drab colors tending toward darker shades. When they trade with humans or other societies on the Material Plane, they often wear masks or concealing clothing to hide their appearance.

 Typical fetchling communities are insular and swiftly close ranks in the event of an intruder. Though individual fetchlings don't mind traveling to and blending in with other societies to facilitate trade, they often hide or even react defensively if they have their own visitors. When one considers the nature of the other denizens of the Shadow Plane, however, this tendency to assume the worst of interlopers might make sense to some.

 Fetchlings are not as common on the Material Plane; most of those who dwell there are loners by choice who have left their home to seek out adventure elsewhere, exiles who were banished from their homelands, or planar castaways with no ability to return to the Shadow Plane. This last category, the lost and stranded, are the most likely to be found in larger groups. In cities of significant size, one can sometimes even find a small community of a few dozen or more fetchlings living among humans, although they often do so subtly by dyeing their hair, wearing brightly colored clothing, and utilizing layers of makeup to hide their pallid complexions.

Irnakurse are surface elves who [drow](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=37) have subjected to particularly cruel and humiliating fleshwarping practices, as drow take special delight in the torment of their surface-dwelling kin. The process of crafting an irnakurse involves twisting an unfortunate elf into a deformed mass of misplaced limbs, loose flesh, and bony protrusions—parts that should be internal are often left on full display to the world. Irnakurse are subject to mental conditioning that causes them to hate their prior forms, and these creatures prefer to attack surface elves over all other targets. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Irnakurse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=670) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 118 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); can't speak any language **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20 **Str** +5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +18 **Will** +16 **HP** 152 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 15 feet **Melee** jaws +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+11 piercing **Melee** tentacle +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus mind lash **Mind Lash** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A non-evil creature hit by an irnakurse's tentacle is overwhelmed with corrupted images of a ruined life and must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or be [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (or stunned 4 on a critical failure). After attempting this save, a creature is temporarily immune to Mind Lash for 24 hours. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** tentacle **Soul Scream** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The irnakurse unleashes an alien shriek of nightmarish horror and pain. All non-evil creatures within a 10-foot emanation must attempt a DC 28 Will save. The irnakurse can Sustain Soul Scream for up to 6 rounds; each time it does, it repeats the effect.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, and is temporarily immune to Soul Scream for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 1. Further failed saves against Soul Scream increase the condition value by 1, to a maximum of stupefied 4. Each time the character gets a full night's rest, the stupefied condition gained from Soul Scream decreases by 1.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the stupefied value increases by 2 instead of by 1. **Storm of Tentacles** The irnakurse makes up to four tentacle Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the irnakurse's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all of its attacks.
Irnakurse Mockeries When drow capture a particularly hated surface elf, or one with a large following, they inflict even greater pain upon them during the fleshwarping process than most other victims. The resulting irnakurse are known as “mockeries,” and always have [elite adjustments](/Rules.aspx?ID=790). Rather than using them, drow keep them close at hand as living “art” akin to a pet or trophy.
name: Irnakurse

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ghonhatine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=671)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In transforming [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102), [drow](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=37) fleshwarpers sought not to create a new type of creature but to recapture a form of xulgath predecessor believed entirely vanished from Golarion. Ghonhatines are the result—hulking reptilian beasts who prefer to crawl on all fours and tear at their foes with bestial fury.

[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)

acid 10

**Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 30 feet. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (plus [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). While within the aura, affected creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +23 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d12+10 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent acid](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d10+10 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
vomit +19 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 5d6 acid plus fleshgout

**Feed** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirement** The ghonhatine is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour; **Effect** The ghonhatine devours a chunk of the corpse. For 1 minute, the ghonhatine gains fast healing 5 and a +2 status bonus to damage rolls. It can gain these benefits from any given corpse only once.

**Fleshgout**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) A ghonhatine's vomit carries an awful disease that, over time, can cause a suffering creature's flesh to develop painful boils that eventually slough away, leaving gaping wounds; **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1d4 hours); **Stage 2** [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 2 and drained 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** enfeebled 2 and drained 3 (1 day); **Stage 5** drained 4, enfeebled 2, and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (1 day); **Stage 6** dead

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Zevgavizeb's Favored</title>

The demonic patron of many [xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=102), [Zevgavizeb](/Deities.aspx?ID=82), spends most of his time slumbering in the Abyssal realm of Gluttondark. However, his vile priests actively pursue what they insist are their dormant god's goals. Some xulgath high priests practice fleshwarping upon their own acolytes, creating [ghonhatine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=671) zealots to serve in their legions and guard their evil temples.
</aside>

**Fly Pox**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162)) A giant fly could carry any disease, but most transmit a virulent but not fatal infection called Fly Pox with their bite; **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 day; **Stage 1** [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 day); **Stage 2** as stage 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** as stage 3; **Stage 5** enfeebled 2 and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) (1 day)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Fly Species</title>

The giant fly presented here is but the most common of these unsettlingly oversized pests. Others can grow much larger, such as the blood-drinking glutton fly, the highly toxic and foul-smelling blight fly, and the immensely bloated plague fly—a nauseating creature ironically incapable of flight that fattens itself on undead flesh and carries a host of awful diseases.
</aside>

name: Giant Fly

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Froghemoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=676)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A terrifying abomination of teeth, tentacles, and unbridled rage, the froghemoth is thankfully rarely encountered even in the deepest swamplands or most remote Darklands caverns. Capable of devouring dinosaurs and even dragons, it is a frighteningly effective ambush hunter, immersing itself in the mire with only its eyestalk protruding and watching in every direction at once with uncanny precision.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Froghemoth.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Froghemoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=676)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=207" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 122

**Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), otherworldly vision 30 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +6

**Con** +7

**Int** -4

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Otherworldly Vision** A froghemoth's alien eyes allow it to perceive creatures within 30 feet, even if they are invisible or ethereal. It can still be fooled by successful [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), but it has Perception DC 38 in such cases. It also sees through and is unaffected by illusions with the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait if they are within 30 feet.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 all-around vision

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +23 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 285

</row>

**Immunities**
electricity

**Electric Torpor** Though a froghemoth is immune to electricity damage, it is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round whenever it would have otherwise taken electricity damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d10+14 piercing plus barbed tongue

**[Aquatic Ambush](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=2)** <actions string="Single Action" />  

**Barbed Tongue**   A creature hit by the froghemoth's tongue becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the froghemoth. The creature isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), but it can't move beyond the reach of the froghemoth's tongue. A creature can sever the tongue with a successful Strike against AC 31 that deals at least 15 slashing damage. This deals no damage to the froghemoth but prevents it from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 minute.

**Flailing Tentacles** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The froghemoth makes up to four tentacle Strikes, each against a different target. These count toward the froghemoth's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.

**[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** <actions string="Single Action" />  3d6+12, DC 33

**Retract Tongue** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The froghemoth pulls a creature grabbed by its tongue toward itself. The froghemoth rolls an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. On a success, the froghemoth pulls the creature into an adjacent space, and if it critically succeeds it can also make a bite Strike against the creature after the pull.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Large, 3d6+9 bludgeoning, Rupture 24

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Alien Origins</title>

Froghemoths have been known to suddenly abandon an established lair to seek a new home, as if in response to some cosmic convergence or subspace signal imperceptible to other creatures. Some argue that the froghemoth is never fully comfortable in any environment because it isn't from this world at all, and that these wanderings are instinctual urges to seek out its true home—a home not represented by the strange world in which the beast finds itself trapped.
</aside>
name: Froghemoth

The frost worm's single round, red eye gleams prominently at the head of its monstrous mass, white as a winter snowfall. What appears to be a bifurcated lower jaw is actually two spiky parapodia that the worm uses to shovel food into its lamprey-like mouth. An adult frost worm measures 35 feet long and weighs 8,000 pounds. A frost worm is an apex predator that uses camouflage, burrowing, and deceit to ambush its prey. When it attacks, a frost worm produces a distinctive, high-pitched trill. Though some survivors of frost worm attacks have compared it to a wailing lament, the trill has a captivating effect on nearby creatures, causing them to stop and listen even as the monstrous creature approaches. The cold shed by a frost worm is as dangerous as its trill or its jaws, and while it is not fond of warmer climates, neither is it particularly hampered in such regions. A frost worm that somehow finds itself in temperate or even tropical environs causes the immediate area to chill and ice over with frost over time, creating seemingly impossible pockets of frozen terrain. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Frost Worm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=677) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 123 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +22 **Will** +20 **HP** 225 **Immunities** cold **Weaknesses** fire 15 **Death Throes** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) When a frost worm dies, it explodes in a 100-foot burst of searing cold blood. All creatures and objects in range take 10d8 cold damage (DC 29 basic Reflex save).
**Worm Chill** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 5 feet. A creature that enters or begins its turn in the emanation takes 3d6 cold damage (DC 29 basic Reflex save).
**Freezing Blood** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) **Trigger** The frost worm takes piercing or slashing damage; **Effect** The frost worm's freezing blood sprays out on a random creature within 10 feet of the frost worm. That creature takes 7d6 cold damage (DC 29 basic Reflex save). --- **Speed** 25 feet, burrow 10 feet **Melee** jaws +25, **Damage** 3d8+10 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent cold](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Breath Weapon** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The frost worm exhales a blast of frost, dealing 13d6 cold damage to creatures in a 50-foot cone (DC 32 basic Reflex save). It can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Worm Trill** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The frost worm emits a hypnotic trill. Each non–frost worm creature within 100 feet must attempt a DC 32 Will save. The effects of Worm Trill last for 1 round, but if the frost worm uses this ability again on subsequent rounds, it extends this duration by 1 round for any creature already affected.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is immune to Worm Trill for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the frost worm.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the fascinated condition doesn't end if the frost worm uses hostile actions against the creature.
Unknown Origins Frost worms are clearly supernatural in origin, though the source of their magical abilities remains unclear. Research is hampered by the fact that neither the corpse nor its magic survive the creature's death, and live frost worms are notoriously difficult and dangerous to keep or transport. Evidence exists that the winter witches of Irrisen may have created the first frost worms, while other clues suggest they hail from the distant planet of Triaxus.
name: Frost Worm

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Wood Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=678)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wood giants are the denizens and protectors of the deepest, most primeval forests in the world. They dedicate their entire lives to the preservation of nature—a role that they believe nature itself has selected them to carry out. Wood giants are patient and kind to those who show respect and care for wild places. Although most wood giants consider civilization as simply another part of the world, they value places untouched by permanent structures and dense humanoid settlements.

 Wood giants have long, drooping ears and more slender builds than most giants. Despite their size, they are graceful and agile, able to move through dense forests and brush with little sound. They have been known to work closely with druids and others who share their respect for the sanctity of wilderness regions. Wood giants spend their days patrolling vast swaths of territory, foraging, and keeping an eye out for any who would despoil their lands. While they respect the connection to nature they share with many fey creatures and remain on good terms with them, wood giants see most fey as far too flighty to take part in any long-term plans.

 An average wood giant stands 14 feet tall and weighs 1,200 pounds.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Giant_WoodGiant.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Wood Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=678)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Giant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=79" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 124

**Perception** +15; low-light vision

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +14, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +5

**Con** +4

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**
[Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), [_+1 composite longbow _](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74) (20 arrows), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 120

</row>

**[Catch Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=7)** <actions string="Reaction" />

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d8+11 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d6+11 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_composite longbow_ +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)),
**Damage** 1d8+8 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +17 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 23
- **2nd**
[Entangle](/Spells.aspx?ID=103), [Tree Shape](/Spells.aspx?ID=342)
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94)
- **4th**
[Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294)
- **Constant (2nd)**
[Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293)

**Piercing Shot** <actions string="Single Action" />  The wood giant shoots an arrow at a target so that it glances off the first target to strike another target behind the first one. The wood giant chooses two targets in a direct line, one of which must be giving lesser cover to the other, then makes a single ranged Strike with its longbow against both targets. This attack ignores the lesser cover the first target provides to the second. The wood giant rolls damage only once, and applies it to each creature it hits. Piercing Shot counts as two attacks for the wood giant's multiple attack penalty.

**[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)** <actions string="Single Action" />  

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Wood Giant Celebrations</title>

Despite the seriousness with which wood giants approach their duties, or perhaps because of it, wood giant gatherings are joyous occasions. Wood giants most often gather to sing, dance, seek life partners, and celebrate their hard work.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-53" />
name: Wood Giant

<traits>
<trait label="CG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />

markdown:
Dwellers of brackish coastal salt marshes and fetid bogs and swamps, marsh giants are hideous in appearance indeed, with fishlike mouths, slimy gray-green skin, and dark, beady eyes. They prefer to eat the flesh of those they slay in battle, but many are also cannibals. It is perhaps this vicious behavior that keeps the creatures in check, for if a fellow marsh giant becomes too dangerous and powerful, it runs the risk of being ambushed and consumed by its clan. Marsh giants are insular and mysterious, dedicating much of their lives to zealous worship of sea-dwelling deities or stranger entities. While there is no central religion followed by all marsh giants, many are known to venerate the demon lord [Dagon](/Deities.aspx?ID=75), while others worship even more powerful monstrosities from the Elder Mythos, such as distant, dreaming Cthulhu. Only rarely do more than a dozen marsh giants settle together in a location, but when they do it is often for a religious purpose. Whatever call from the deep draws them together also tugs at nearby creatures such as boggards or skum, who look upon marsh giants in fear and reverence. The grim, terrifying chants and screams coming from such an encampment are a sure sign of terror to come for any other residents of the area. Barely topping 11 feet tall and 1,000 pounds, marsh giants are small for giants but make up for their relatively diminutive stature with their zealotry. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Marsh Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=679) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 125 **Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [gaff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=121)_, [sack with 5 rocks](/Equipment.aspx?ID=45) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 150 **[Catch Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=7)** --- **Speed** 35 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** gaff +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d6+14 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+14 bludgeoning **Ranged** rock +20 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+14 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 23 - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) - **5th** [Mariner's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=184) **Hook Shake** **Requirements** A creature adjacent to the marsh giant is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) in water at least 1 foot deep; **Effect** The marsh giant uses its gaff to shake the creature back and forth and hold it underwater. The giant attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the target's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** The target takes 6d6+14 piercing damage and loses 5 rounds' worth of air if they were [holding their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).
**Success** The target takes 3d6+14 piercing damage and loses 3 rounds' worth of air if they were holding their breath.
**Failure** The target is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the giant drops its gaff as well. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)** **Twist the Hook** The marsh giant makes a melee Strike with its gaff. If it hits, it twists and yanks the gaff to create an awful wound, dealing 3d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to the creature.
Gaffs Many marsh giants fight with oversized [gaffs](/Weapons.aspx?ID=121)—lengths of wood with a single metal spike affixed to the tip. Used often by fisherfolk to land fish, marsh giants use their gaffs as weapons. A gaff sized for a Medium creature is a common martial weapon in the club group. It deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage and has 1 Bulk. It requires one hand to use and has the [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196) and [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) weapon traits. Gaffs are readily available in fishing or coastal settlements for 1 gp.
name: Marsh Giant

markdown:
Taiga giants prefer a nomadic lifestyle, both to keep from depleting any one area's resources while satiating their massive appetites and to satisfy a constant wanderlust. They are deeply spiritual and frequently commune with their ancestors' spirits for guidance and knowledge. Taiga giants are happiest when they are left alone to live out their traditional lives, and their impressive size and strength are enough to persuade all but the most dangerous foes to do so. Taiga giants subsist on migratory herds of aurochs, mammoths, and elk. They occasionally raid humanoid villages, but such cases are opportunistic rather than malicious, with a focus on stealing away livestock for food rather than people. Nevertheless, the arrival of a group of taiga giants is a potentially devastating event to any town or village, so many communities attempt to placate the giants by creating a yearly offering at those times when they know the giants are scheduled to come close. Most taiga giants venerate their ancestors and seek to honor their works in everything they do. This deep connection makes them ferociously proud of those legacies, and little can compel taiga giants to go to war more swiftly than insulting their ancestors. Merely mentioning the false rumors that ancient taiga giant spellcasters were responsible for the creation of the first rune giants is a surefire way to earn a taiga giant's unending wrath. A taiga giant stands 20 feet tall and weighs 10,000 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Taiga Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=680) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 126 **Perception** +23; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), see invisibility **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Genealogy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +25 **Str** +7 **Dex** +1 **Con** +5 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **See Invisibility** A taiga giant can see invisible creatures and objects as translucent shapes, and they are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to the taiga giant. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=6)_, [sack with 5 rocks](/Equipment.aspx?ID=45) --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +20 **Will** +22 +2 status to all saves vs. enchantment and illusion effects **HP** 230 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) **[Catch Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=7)
**Guardian Spirit **Trigger** The taiga giant has Ancestral Guardian active and would take energy or mental damage; **Effect** The taiga giant's ancestral spirits intervene and protect the giant from taking up to 20 energy damage or 30 mental damage. The giant takes any remaining damage; if it does, the spirits depart and the giant is no longer protected by the ancestors. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _longspear_ +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+15 piercing **Melee** fist +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+15 bludgeoning **Ranged** rock +25 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+15 bludgeoning **Ancestral Guardian** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Requirements** The taiga giant must be protected by the ancestors; **Effect** The taiga giant calls upon their ancestors' spirits to rise up and protect them, causing a cloak of spectral faces to shimmer and swirl around them. The taiga giant's AC increases to 34 until the start of its next turn. **Protected by the Ancestors** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) A taiga giant's prayers to their ancestors grant them spiritual protection. If a taiga giant loses this protection (such as by taking too much damage when using Guardian Spirit, or if the ancestors are counteracted by [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78)), it loses its immunity to the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) condition and its status bonus to saving throws against enchantment and illusion effects. A taiga giant can once again be Protected by the Ancestors by performing a 10-minute prayer as an activity that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)**
Taiga Giant Clans Each taiga giant clan links its identity to the deeds of their most famous ancestors and heroes. Names such as Cliffsmiter and Mountainripper hint at legendary accomplishments, though the stories are rarely shared with outsiders.
name: Taiga Giant
