<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Jellyfish](/Monsters.aspx?ID=707)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Unlike its smaller cousins, the giant jellyfish is an active predator that chases down its prey through reefs or open water. It can even squeeze its enormous bell-shaped body into the tight confines of shipwrecks to drape its mane of tentacles across the exposed flesh of its prey.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Jellyfish_GiantJellyfish.png" />

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Jyoti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=708)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Jyotis are sometimes called “false phoenixes” (a term they find insulting) by the ignorant or willful. These avian humanoids are native to the [Positive Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=3). They rarely leave this enigmatic realm and view visitors from other planes as stains on the purity of their home. Jyotis particularly distrust divine spellcasters and religious warriors, seeing them as inclined to take credit for manifesting a life force that, from the jyotis' perspective, is as plentiful and ubiquitous as water is to fish.

 Despite their distrust of intruders, jyotis rarely attack unprovoked when their homes are not threatened. They are intolerant, however, of those who intrude on the palaces of crystallized light and captured flame in which they dwell. They often chase even those who come bearing gifts away; few visitors have anything they desire, for what they desire most is to be left alone. The jyotis' full wrath is reserved for natives of the [Shadow Plane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=288) and the [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2). Historically, the promise of battle with the gargoyle-like [sceaduinars](/Monsters.aspx?ID=794), whom they consider it their duty to oppose, has been the only thing to lure jyoti armies beyond the Positive Energy Plane.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Jyoti.png" />
</column>

</column>

<actions string="Single Action" />
_[flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) longspear_ +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+6 piercing plus 1d6 fire

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +21 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),

talon +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d8+6 slashing plus 1d6 fire

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Disrupt Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=79), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171)
- **2nd**
[Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) (×3)
- **3rd**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×3)
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Searing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=269)
- **5th**
[Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=19), [Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=29)

**Breath Weapon**  ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The jyoti breathes a blast of searing flame infused with positive energy in a 40-foot cone that deals 8d6 fire damage plus 4d6 positive damage to creatures in the area (DC 28 basic Reflex save). The jyoti can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

**Infuse Weapons**  ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Any weapon a jyoti wields becomes a _[flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297)_ weapon while the jyoti holds it.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Crystal Vaults</title>

Jyoti make ideal guardians for artifacts too dangerous to be left where those hungry for power might seize them. Only they know what items of myth and legend, long thought lost or destroyed, lie within their crystal vaults. They are unreliable guardians of religious artifacts, however, which usually disgust them.
</aside>
name: Jyoti

Kelpies are malevolent amphibious fey shapechangers intent on luring mortals to their doom. These cruel predators lurk in and around areas of water, slightly preferring freshwater over saltwater. Kelpies lure or drag their prey underwater then drown and devour them, leaving behind only the victim's heart and liver— the only parts of a meal kelpies find unpleasant. Kelpies are fond of magically disguising itself as fine steeds or attractive strangers to draw in victims, but its true appearance takes the form of a hideous equine with slimy, green flesh resembling aquatic plants. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Kelpie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=709) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 154 **Perception** +11; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** -1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +12 **Will** +14 **HP** 60 **Resistances** fire 5 **Weaknesses** cold iron 5 --- **Speed** 35 feet, swim 35 feet **Melee** jaws +13, **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Captivating Lure** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The kelpie instills an overwhelming attraction to itself within the mind of a single creature within 60 feet. The target perceives the kelpie as a desirable person (if the kelpie is in humanoid form) or a valuable steed (if the kelpie is in equine form) and must attempt a DC 23 Will saving throw.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Captivating Lure for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round and is then temporarily immune to Captivating Lure for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), and it must spend each of its actions to move closer to the kelpie as expediently as possible while avoiding obvious dangers. If a captivated creature is adjacent to the kelpie, it either attempts to mount the kelpie (if the kelpie is in equine form) or stays still and doesn't act. If the creature is attacked by the kelpie, or if it can't breathe water and enters an area of water, the creature is freed from captivation at the end of the kelpie's turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target doesn't consider water a danger and will enter an area of water even if it can't swim or breathe water. If it is attacked by the kelpie or starts to drown, it can attempt a new save at the start of its next turn, but it isn't freed automatically. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The kelpie can take on the appearance of any Medium or Large animal of an equine nature (such as a horse, [hippocampus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=696), or pony), or any Small or Medium humanoid. This doesn't change its Speeds or its attack and damage modifiers with its Strikes.
Kelpie Folktales Some fanciful stories about kelpies speak of them appearing in equine form wearing riding tack, complete with silver stirrups and bridle bit. These folktales claim that cutting the harness from the kelpie's body grants the bearer power over it, or causes the kelpie to sicken and die. In truth, doing so has no ill effect on a kelpie, suggesting these stories are spread by kelpies themselves to further trick prey into making foolish mistakes.
name: Kelpie

Korreds are reclusive fey in the form of small, hirsute individuals with long, animated manes of hair. While korreds have a natural affinity and admiration for stones and boulders, they prefer to dwell aboveground in areas of rocky forest or wooded hills rather than in caves. Korreds are a private people who don't take kindly to intruders, and they invariably work to drive off or kill any non-fey who stumble into their territory. Staunchly proud of their hair, korreds choose their minimal attire so as to allow their body hair to flow freely, often wearing only a belt with a pouch for throwing rocks and trimming shears—though this belt and pouch are rarely visible under their wild manes. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Korred](/Monsters.aspx?ID=710) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 155 **Perception** +12; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 **Items** [Club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=2), pouch with 5 rocks --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +13 **Will** +10 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 65 **Weaknesses** cold iron 5 **Animated Hair** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) 5 feet. The korred's long, animated hair reaches out and interferes with creatures in the area. The korred can select which targets are affected by their animated hair. An affected creature that ends its turn in the emanation must succeed at a DC 18 Reflex save or become [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3)(clumsy 2 on a critical failure) as long as it remains in the aura. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** club +14, **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning **Ranged** rock +14 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+7 bludgeoning **Ranged** club +14 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 21 - **4th** [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276) (at will), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) (at will) - **6th** [Stone Tell](/Spells.aspx?ID=310) **Hair Snare** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The korred causes a long, tangled length of their hair to detach from their body and snake out to coil around an adjacent creature. The creature must attempt a DC 21 Reflex save. The korred cannot use Hair Snare again for 1d4 rounds.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The hair wraps around the creature. The creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all of its Speeds until it Escapes (DC 21) or until the korred uses Hair Snare again (at which point the previous snare drops to the ground, no longer animated).
**Critical Failure** The creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it Escapes (DC 21) or the korred uses Hair Snare again. **Otherworldly Laugh** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The korred unleashes an otherworldly laugh. Each non-[fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) creature within a 30-foot burst must attempt a DC 21 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round (or [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) on a critical failure). **Stone Stride** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The korred steps into a block of stone large enough for them to fit inside and instantly teleports to any other stone within 30 feet that has the same minimum size. Once the korred enters the stone, they instantly know the rough locations of other sufficiently large stones within 30 feet. They can exit from the original stone, if they prefer. They can't carry extradimensional spaces with them when they Stone Stride; if the korred attempts to do so, Stone Stride fails. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)**
Korred Dances Despite their insular nature, korreds love to dance. On certain auspicious dates, korreds hold great festivals of music and dance in ancient stone circles deep within forest glades. A few non-korred fey sometimes receive invitations to these dances, but any non-fey who interrupts the dance is berated at best or attacked at worst.
name: Korred

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Leech](/Monsters.aspx?ID=711)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Capable of growing to lengths of nearly 5 feet, giant leeches have circular maws filled with hook-like teeth. They prefer to dwell in stagnant or slow-moving shallow water or in damp, moist undergrowth. Horses and larger animals are their favorite prey, but they won't balk at a chance to latch onto a human- or halfling-sized meal.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Brood Leech Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=712)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Most smaller species of leeches do not tend to swarm, but brood leeches are prone to gathering in seething, undulant mats of squirming gluttony. When they gather in sufficient numbers to swarm, they eschew the stealth of a lone leech's feeding methods in favor of swift and merciless feeding. In these situations, their mild venom can affect much larger creatures than their usual prey.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Leng Spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=713)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The monstrous, bloated spiders from the windswept realm of Leng build eerie, dangerous lairs with the aid of magically compelled slaves. Leng spiders have anywhere from five to 13 legs, but never an even number.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 33

**[Recall Knowledge - Dream](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/LengSpider.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Leng Spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=713)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
<trait label="Dream" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=299" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 157

**Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), greater web sense

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +24, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +7

**Con** +5

**Int** +7

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Greater Web Sense** While touching its webs, the Leng spider has precise [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) to detect the vibrations of creatures touching its web.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 34 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +24 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 235
([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 10)

</row>

**Immunities**
cold, [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5)

**Resistances**
poison 15, sonic 15

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
web war flail +27 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 3d10+14 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus Leng spider venom

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
leg +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+14 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
web bola +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Ranged Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=251), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d6+14 bludgeoning

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 33
- **Cantrips (7th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66)
- **4th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (×3), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128)
- **6th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161), [Mislead](/Spells.aspx?ID=199)
- **7th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369)
- **Constant (7th)**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Create Web Weaponry** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The Leng spider creates a weapon by applying a cord of webbing to heavy objects, such as rocks or chunks of metal, either attaching two heavy objects together to create a web [bola](/Weapons.aspx?ID=92) or fastening one to its leg to create a web [war flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=46).

**Descend on a Web** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The Leng spider moves straight down up to 120 feet, suspended by a web line. It can hang from the web or drop off. The distance it Descends on a Web doesn't count when calculating falling damage. The web can be severed by a Strike that deals slashing damage (AC 30, Hardness 15, 25 HP), causing the Leng spider to fall.

**Lay Web Trap** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The Leng spider spins a web within 20 feet of itself to create a [grasping snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=334), [stunning snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=346), or [warning snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=348). The Leng spider's web provides all the raw materials it needs. All save and [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DCs associated with web traps use the Leng spider's [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) DC for traps (DC 36). A web trap decays after 24 hours.

**Leng Spider Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 33 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage, [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), and drained 2 (1 round); the confused effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits.

**Ranged Trip**   A Leng spider can use a web bola to [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196) a target with the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) skill. The skill check takes a –2 circumstance penalty. A web bola deals no damage when used to Trip.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Web Temples</title>

Leng spiders build complex lairs out of their webs, many of which serve a dual purpose as temples to the eldritch gods they venerate. Most Leng spiders worship deities of the Elder Mythos, such as [Hastur](/Deities.aspx?ID=162), [Nyarlathotep](/Deities.aspx?ID=164), or [Yog-Sothoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=166), among others.
</aside>
name: Leng Spider

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Leprechaun](/Monsters.aspx?ID=714)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Leprechauns are mostly jovial tricksters who prefer mischief over conflict. They fill their days with as much fun, wine, and food as possible. Mostly found in forested regions, leprechauns respect nature and those who protect it.

 Leprechauns do not attack on sight. Rather, they engage in conversation and try to charm, cajole, or trick those they meet into doing favors for them or freely giving over a treasured item, usually in return for illusory wealth or false promises of wealth and success. These small tricksters are masters at discerning the desires of those they meet—a knack that puts them in a powerful position when bargaining for goods or favors. They aren't above turning people against each other for their own benefit but generally not to an extent that causes harm.

 In most cases, a leprechaun doesn't keep a purloined possession for long. The leprechaun most often returns such stolen prizes just in time to defuse tensions, often as they point out the humor of the situation, hoping to share their amusement and mirth with the victim. In cases where a leprechaun's trick goes too far and results in an incensed victim, the leprechaun quickly flees the conflict rather than engage in combat. This willingness to return stolen goods or to flee from battles fades as leprechauns grow older. Ancient leprechauns who have lived for thousands of years often spiral into dark bitterness and increasingly use their powers and illusions to lure those who offend them or fail to appreciate a joke into danger... or even death.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Leprechaun.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Leprechaun](/Monsters.aspx?ID=714)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 158

**Perception** +11; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +9, [Gold Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +7, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +7, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +9, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +3

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +4
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 25

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
club +7,
**Damage** 1d6+3 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +10
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **1st**
[Color Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=44), [Shillelagh](/Spells.aspx?ID=282), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356)
- **2nd**
[Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (self only)

**Create Object** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The leprechaun produces an item out of their hat, from behind their jacket, from within a hole in a tree stump, or from any other unexpected location. This conjured item must be no more than 1 Bulk and must be made of relatively commonplace material (such as cloth, wood, stone, or even low-value metal like iron or lead). It can't rely on intricate artistry or complex moving parts, never fulfills a Cost or the like, and can't be made of precious materials or materials with a rarity of [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) or higher. The created object is temporary and lasts for 1 hour or until the leprechaun creates a new item, whichever comes first.

**Leprechaun Magic**   When a leprechaun uses their innate spells to deceive, trick, or humiliate a creature, the spell DC increases to 20 and the attack modifier to +11.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Pots of Gold</title>

While it's true that leprechauns typically return items they steal, they particularly love gold and often hoard gold coins and treasures in pots tucked away in hidden places. It's rumored that a person who finds a gold coin in the forest and returns it to the leprechaun who dropped it will be granted a wish as a reward. Unfortunately, this rumor is false—a deception perpetrated by leprechauns to trick others into bringing them even more gold for their pots.
</aside>
name: Leprechaun

Lerritans are unstoppable giants with skin of volcanic glass and blood of roiling lava. These malicious arsonists would see the world burned to a cinder. They pay homage to [Ymeri](/Deities.aspx?ID=108), the evil elemental demigod of fire, and they bully other fiery creatures. When the urge to immolate grows too great to ignore, lerritans burn forests, destroy villages, and boil rivers, but they avoid large bodies of water. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Lerritan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=715) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 159 **Perception** +35; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +41, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +35, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +36, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +38 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** +2 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +4 **Items** _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=47)_ --- **AC** 46 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +34 **Will** +36 **HP** 490 **Immunities** fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** piercing 20, slashing 20 **Weaknesses** cold 20 **Tenacious Flames** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 100 feet. Creatures in the emanation
cannot recover from [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 50 feet **Melee** warhammer +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 4d12+18 bludgeoning plus 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** jaws +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+18 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** rock +39 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d6+18 bludgeoning plus 2d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 46 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (to the Material Plane, [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6), or [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) only) - **8th** [Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=95) - **9th** [Meteor Swarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=191), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) - **10th** [Cataclysm](/Spells.aspx?ID=32) **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=38)** A lerritan can break stony scales off its body to throw; these scales reform at the end of each round, so the lerritan is never without a supply of rocks to hurl. **Volcanic Eruption** ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The volcano on the lerritan's back erupts and sends lava bombs raining down in a 30-foot emanation, dealing 12d12 fire damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 46 Reflex saving throw. The lava globules quickly cool into heavy stones, transforming the area into greater difficult terrain for non-lerritans. The lerritan can't use Volcanic Eruption for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) (Escape DC 46).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is encased in a rocky crust with lava on the inside. The creature is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) (Escape DC 46), can't breathe, takes 3d12 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and can't recover from this persistent fire damage until freed.
Lerritan Cavalcades Every few decades, lerritans gather in large bands to travel from one volcanic region to another, razing every sign of civilization they find along the way. Lerritan cavalcades contain up to a dozen lerritans and their lackeys: [crimson worms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=75), powerful [fire elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=43), and [fire giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=223)
name: Lerritan

markdown:
Sunflower leshys are the ambassadors and social leaders of their kind. Their petals radiate from their heads in various hues with a serrated, leafy, beard-like ruff. When particularly excited, their eyes and petals glow with collected sunlight, and when they become depressed or saddened, their usually vibrant coloration grows correspondingly muted. These shifts in coloration are echoed in sunflower leshy art, which can lead to unexpected color palettes for artworks. For example, a human artist might illustrate Hell as a place of glowing lava and flickering flames, drawing upon many vibrant colors, but to a sunflower leshy, the only proper way to depict a place like Hell is in morose grays, blacks, and whites. The more adventurous sunflower leshys leave their communities to grow bonds with nearby settlements of humanoids. When interacting with humanoids, sunflower leshys are some of the most likely to act friendly and give strangers a chance to prove their good intentions, though they remain cautious. They're often accompanied by other, more powerful leshys. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Sunflower Leshy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=716) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 160 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Druidic](/Languages.aspx?ID=23), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) (sunflowers only) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +4 **Ref** +10 **Will** +7 **HP** 20 **Heliotrope** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 20 feet; **Requirements** The sunflower leshy begins its turn in an area of bright light; **Effect** The sunflower leshy reflects the sun or another source of bright light from their face. Each creature that ends its turn in the emanation must attempt a DC 16 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to heliotrope for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature is distracted by the light, becoming [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Verdant Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89)) When a sunflower leshy dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from its body, restoring 1d8 Hit Points to each [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area immediately fills with sunflowers, becoming difficult terrain. If the terrain is not a viable environment for these sunflowers, they wither after 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** tendril +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning **Ranged** seed +6 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **4th** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The sunflower leshy transforms into a Small flower. This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=342). **Seed Spray** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The sunflower leshy launches a deluge of seeds from their head in a 15-foot cone, dealing 2d6 bludgeoning damage to creatures within the area (DC 16 basic Reflex save). It gains a +2 status bonus to this damage against [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) creatures. The sunflower leshy can't use Seed Spray again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Sunflower Leshy

markdown:
While often seen as unfriendly by non-leshys, flytrap leshys get along well with other leshys. Nonetheless, they're among the most aggressive leshys, often guarding the most vulnerable places in the natural world with their flytrap mouths and hands. When a large threat emerges, pairs of flytrap leshys band together to create amalgams capable of driving back powerful foes. This unusual form of communal defense suffuses flytrap leshy society, and they often form relationships between multiple individuals that would confuse or even scandalize more uptight humanoids—to a flytrap leshy, though, there's nothing strange about sharing your innermost secrets with people you literally merged together with to defend your home from an enemy. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Flytrap Leshy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=717) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 161 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Druidic](/Languages.aspx?ID=23), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) (carnivorous plants only) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +12 **Will** +13 **HP** 72 **Resistances** acid 5 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Verdant Burst** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89)) When a flytrap leshy dies, a burst of primal energy explodes from its body, restoring 3d6 Hit Points to each [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature in a 30-foot emanation. This area immediately fills with flytraps, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). If the terrain is not a viable environment for these flytraps, they wither after 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** flytrap mouth +13 ([versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d8+6 piercing plus 1d6 acid and flytrap toxin **Melee** flytrap hand +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6+6 piercing plus 1d6 acid and flytrap toxin **Ranged** spittle +11 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [range increment 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+6 acid plus flytrap toxin **Primal Innate Spells** DC 21 - **2nd** [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215) - **4th** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Amalgam** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) A flytrap leshy can combine itself with an adjacent and willing flytrap leshy that is not currently affected by Amalgam. The leshy using Amalgam physically merges with the target, restoring 3d8 Hit Points to the target. The leshy can Sustain a Spell to continue Amalgam, but once they stop, the target leshy takes 3d8 damage. If the target leshy dies, Amalgam ends at once and the original leshy gains the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) condition or increases their dying condition value by 1 if they were already dying. As long as Amalgam is sustained, the target leshy gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws, its AC increases to 21, and it gains an additional reaction at the start of each turn. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The flytrap leshy transforms into a Small flytrap. This ability otherwise uses the effects of [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=342). **Flytrap Toxin** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (1 round), **Stage 2** sickened 2 (1 round)
name: Flytrap Leshy

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Leucrotta](/Monsters.aspx?ID=718)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The vile and vicious offspring of a demon lord and a monstrous species of hyenas, leucrottas are intelligent and cruel beasts. They use their astounding vocal mimicry to lure unsuspecting creatures near so that they can first torment and then devour them. Leucrottas measure 5 feet at the shoulder and weigh 800 pounds, and they are perpetually filthy. A leucrotta's oversized jaws are lined with rows of jagged, hardened bony ridges rather than teeth, and it can shear through flesh, bone, and even steel with ease.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Leucrotta.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Leucrotta](/Monsters.aspx?ID=718)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 162

**Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); one additional language (usually Gnoll)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Language Adaptation** A leucrotta can learn any language it hears spoken for at least 10 minutes, adding it to its languages known and replacing the language it previously learned using this ability. Typically, a leucrotta knows Gnoll as the language granted by this ability.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 85

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 50 feet, climb 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jagged jaws +15 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=178)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning

**Luring Cry** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 60 feet; **Requirements** The leucrotta's last action was Sound Mimicry; **Effect** The leucrotta utters a plaintive cry to draw its prey closer. Each creature within the emanation that was deceived by Sound Mimicry must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or become **fascinated** and compelled to move toward the sound of the leucrotta's voice on their turn. Fascinated creatures are also **flat-footed**. If the leucrotta attacks, the fascinated condition ends only for the creature that is attacked. If the leucrotta speaks a creature's name while using its Luring Cry, that creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to their saving throw to resist the ability. On a successful save, a creature is temporarily immune to Luring Cry for 24 hours.

**Sound Mimicry** <actions string="Single Action" />  The leucrotta perfectly imitates voices and speech and attempts a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against listeners' Will DC to fool them. The leucrotta gains a +4 bonus to this Deception check if it has listened to the creature it's imitating for at least 10 minutes at any point in the last 24 hours. The leucrotta can't duplicate voice-based abilities or spells, though it can perfectly mimic the sound of verbal spellcasting and can attempt to deceive [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=35) or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) that respond to voice commands. The leucrotta can't imitate speech in languages it doesn't know.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Gnoll Masters</title>

Leucrottas often associate with gnolls. They never submit to serve others as beasts of burden, but they sometimes permit gnoll leaders who impress them to ride them into battle as steeds. More often, a leucrotta considers itself to be the true leader of a gnoll clan, whispering and manipulating a gnoll warchief into doing its bidding or (if it tires of the game) goading them into challenging the leucrotta for leadership and then taking over the clan directly after eliminating its rival.
</aside>
name: Leucrotta

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Leydroth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=719)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In ancient times, a cabal of primalists imbued an amalgam of beasts with both supernatural might and a hatred of magic itself, hoping to achieve a potent weapon for use against arcane spellcasters. Effective beyond expectation, the first leydroths broke free and turned on their creators. Worse, the leydroths bred true, and these reclusive predators have long outlived their original creators.

 Leydroths look like distorted, apelike felines with many-branching horns growing from the backs of their heads. Their thickly muscled limbs end in glowing claws.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 38

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Leydroth.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 17">[Leydroth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=719)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 163

**Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), magic sense (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +30

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +7

**Con** +9

**Int** -3

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Magic Sense** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The leydroth detects the source and school of each source of magic within 60 feet as an imprecise sense.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 40 or 36 vs. non-magical

**Fort** +32 

**Ref** +30 

**Will** +28 

+2 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 315

</row>

**Resistances**
all 15 (except non-magical)

**Menace to Magic** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet Casts a Spell; **Effect** The leydroth attempts an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check to counteract the triggering spell.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+16 piercing plus 1d10 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d10+16 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn sweep +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d12+16 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 38
- **7th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (at will)
- **9th**
[Disjunction](/Spells.aspx?ID=77) (×2)

**Dispelling Roar** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The leydroth unleashes a bestial roar that reverberates within a 30-foot emanation. It rolls an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check and compares the result to the Will DC of each creature in the area, with the effects of [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53). It also uses this result to attempt to counteract each spell or magical effect in the area, plus one item or effect on each creature in the area, with the effects of [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78). The leydroth can't use Dispelling Roar again for 1d4 rounds.

**Dispelling Strike** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** The leydroth hits a creature, object, or spell effect with a Strike; **Effect** The leydroth casts its innate [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), targeting one effect on the creature struck.

**Spell Feedback**  ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) Any time a leydroth counteracts a spell or item, the spell's caster or the creature holding the item takes 8d6 mental damage (DC 38 basic Will save).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Leydroth Legacy</title>

On Golarion, the first leydroths were created by a secret society of Azlanti sorcerers who drew their powers from elemental bloodlines. These sorcerers clashed often against various wizardly guilds who held power throughout the various regions of Azlant, and intended for leydroths to serve as weapons of terror against their foes. Today, leydroths remain isolated on certain particularly dangerous islands among the remnants of the shattered continent of Azlant, but now and then foolish explorers seek to transport them back to the mainland.
</aside>
name: Leydroth

markdown:
Shoal linnorms occupy rocky shoreline shallows and slither along lonely coastlines. While they do not intentionally seek out large settlements, these linnorms nonetheless beleaguer fishers headed out from small coastal towns, and they may even follow skiffs back to their home port to appraise the town's defenses and plan a raid. Shoal linnorms tend to hunt large prey, both underwater and on land. Their preferred quarries includes giant squid and grizzly bears—meals rich in fat, which the linnorms metabolize into the fiery steam that courses through their veins. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Shoal Linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=720) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 164 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +25 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 295 , regeneration 10 (deactivated by cold iron) **Immunities** [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** cold iron 10 **Curse of Drowning** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) When a creature slays a shoal linnorm, it must succeed at a DC 38 Will save or become cursed. As long as the curse of drowning persists, the character must spend 3 actions when drinking any liquid. Attempting to drink liquid faster causes the victim to begin [drowning](/Rules.aspx?ID=468), immediately running out of air and falling unconscious.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 100 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+16 piercing plus shoal linnorm venom **Melee** claw +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+16 slashing **Melee** tail +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d6+16 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 36 - **Constant (6th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **Constant (7th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Breath Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) The shoal linnorm exhales scalding steam that deals 12d8 fire damage in a 60-foot cone (DC 36 basic Reflex save). The steam lingers until the end of the linnorm's next turn; anyone who enters the area or begins their turn in the area takes 6d8 fire damage (DC 36 Reflex save negates). During this time, the steam is so thick that it impedes movement as well as sight, turning the area into difficult terrain. All creatures in the steam become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the steam become concealed to creatures within it. The shoal linnorm can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Shoal Linnorm Venom** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 36 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 fire damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** 6d6 fire damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)
name: Shoal Linnorm

markdown:
Fjord linnorms make their homes in damp caves behind ice-cold waterfalls in the river-veined coastal reaches of the north. While they may appear graceful as they swim up the fjords for which they're named, they are as vile as any other of their species and take pleasures in capsizing river boats before gobbling up their terrified crews. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Fjord Linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=721) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 165 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +8 **Int** -3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +28 **Will** +24 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 315 , regeneration 10 (deactivated by cold iron) **Immunities** cold, [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** cold iron 15 **Curse of Stolen Breath** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) When a creature slays a fjord linnorm, it must succeed at a DC 41 Will save or become unable to ever breathe underwater (either via a natural ability or a spell such as [_water breathing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=370)). In addition, the victim can hold its breath only half as long as normal, and whenever it holds its breath it becomes [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 75 feet, swim 50 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing plus fjord linnorm venom **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 slashing **Melee** tail +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+15 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 37 - **Constant (6th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **Constant (7th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Breath Weapon** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The fjord linnorm expels a 120-foot line of icy bile, dealing 17d6 cold damage to creatures within the area (DC 37 basic Reflex save). Any creature that fails its save is covered by the ice, which freezes and fuses with the creature's skin, giving it a –10-foot penalty to Speed. A creature can [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the ice (DC 34) to free itself; otherwise, the ice remains for 1 minute. The fjord linnorm can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Fjord Linnorm Venom** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 37 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 cold damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 round); **Stage 2** 6d6 cold damage and clumsy 2 (1 round)
name: Fjord Linnorm

markdown:
Cairn linnorms are disturbing, even by linnorm standards. They make their homes in necropolises, burial grounds, and the sites of immense and gory battles. Some point out that cairn linnorms feast on shambling undead and thus provide a service to the living, but these beasts are not choosy and will happily consume any creature, whether or not it draws breath. Some tales state that cairn linnorms will not—or cannot—enter a tomb without the permission of a descendant of the deceased (or the permission of the deceased itself, in the instance that it has risen from the dead). Likewise, once a cairn linnorm has entered a tomb, it won't leave until it has secured permission to do so. Whether such legends are true or not is anyone's guess; nevertheless, reports of a cairn linnorm in the vicinity are harrowing enough to dissuade even the most foolhardy grave robber from peeking into too many crypts or mausoleums. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Cairn Linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=722) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 166 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** -2 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +30 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 360 , regeneration 15 (deactivated by cold iron) **Immunities** acid, [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** cold iron 15 **Curse of the Crooked Cane** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) When a creature slays a cairn linnorm, it must succeed at a DC 44 Will save or become permanently [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13). In addition, the victim ages at an accelerated rate, aging 1 year every day, eventually causing it to die of old age if the curse is left untended.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 40 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 40 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing plus cairn linnorm venom **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing **Melee** tail +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 40 - **Constant (7th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **Constant (8th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Breath Weapon** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The cairn linnorm expels a 60-foot cone of negative energy–infused acid, dealing 19d6 acid damage to creatures in the area (DC 40 basic Reflex save). The acid also saps the life out of affected creatures. At the beginning of the linnorm's next turn, each creature that failed the Reflex save must succeed at a DC 40 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 2 on a critical failure). The cairn linnorm can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Cairn Linnorm Venom** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 41 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 6d6 acid damage and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 round); **Stage 2** 8d6 acid damage and drained 2 (1 round)
name: Cairn Linnorm

markdown:
Taiga linnorms are covered in hundreds of quill-like black spines as sharp as spears. Wise hunters native to linnorm-infested lands know to watch for spines stuck in the bark of conifers—a sure sign that a taiga linnorm has recently passed through. Sometimes this means of tracking isn't enough to avoid an encounter, however; taiga linnorms, like their tarn and tor brethren, are skilled stalkers. They prefer to lurk in half-frozen bogs or swampy marshlands with their heads poking just above the water's surface, ever watchful for unwary prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Taiga Linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=723) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 167 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** -2 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +31 **Will** +29 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 385 , regeneration 15 (deactivated by cold iron) **Immunities** [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), electricity, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** cold iron 15 **Curse of Endless Storms** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) When a creature slays a taiga linnorm, it must succeed at a DC 46 Will save or permanently gain weakness 20 to electricity.
**Spines** Any creature that makes a melee attack against a taiga linnorm is stabbed by the taiga linnorm's spines and takes 1d6 piercing damage per attack. A melee weapon with [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) protects the user against these spines.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 50 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus taiga linnorm venom **Melee** claw +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing **Melee** tail +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 5d6+18 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 41 - **Constant (8th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **Constant (9th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Breath Weapon** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The taiga linnorm breathes a 60.foot cone of electrified vapor, dealing 20d6 electricity damage to creatures in the area (DC 41 basic Reflex save). The electrified mist persists for 1d4 rounds, dealing 6d6 electricity damage (DC 41 basic Reflex save) to each creature that ends its turn in the mist. The taiga linnorm can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Taiga Linnorm Venom** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 42 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 7d6 electricity damage and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 round); **Stage 2** 10d6 electricity damage and drained 2 (1 round)
name: Taiga Linnorm

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Chameleon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=724)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Giant chameleons are legendary for their ability to change their skin color in response to their surroundings. Their eyes are capable of peering in different directions independently, making them almost as difficult to sneak up on as they are to notice in the first place.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**

</column>

**Camouflage** The giant chameleon can change its coloration to match its surroundings. It doesn't need cover to attempt to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) with a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check.

<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** tongue grab

**Tongue Grab**   If the giant chameleon hits a creature with a tongue Strike, that creature becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the giant chameleon. The target isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), but it can't move beyond the reach of the giant chameleon's tongue. A creature can sever the tongue with an attack that hits AC 15 and deals at least 4 slashing damage. Though this doesn't deal any damage to the giant chameleon, it prevents it from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes a week.

</column>

markdown:
These malicious and strange fey appear as fairies about 2 feet tall, with insectile wings, large eyes, and bulbous heads. They slaughter and cause mischief in a pattern only their alien intellect understands, but they've been seen to take particular umbrage against dwarves, gnomes, and creatures who live in darkness. With their grudges and pursuit of their own wicked and sadistic whims, lurkers in light deftly overturn the conventional wisdom that creatures associated with light are benign and friendly. While lurkers in light are fey and have ties to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), these creatures are often found elsewhere in the Great Beyond. They are particularly fond of invading the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) to bring light to those who dwell there-not to help, but to spread misery and pain among those to whom light is agonizing. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Lurker In Light](/Monsters.aspx?ID=726) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 169 **Perception** +13; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +13 **Str** +0 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +14 **Will** +13 **HP** 72 **Immunities** [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) **Weaknesses** cold iron 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+2 slashing **Ranged** mote of light +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d4+2 force plus lurker's glow **Primal Innate Spells** DC 22, attack +14 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Searing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=269), [Summon Fey](/Spells.aspx?ID=322) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (only when in bright light, and only to an area in bright light), [Summon Fey](/Spells.aspx?ID=322) **Blend with Light** **Trigger** The lurker in light uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action; **Requirements** The lurker in light is in an area of bright light; **Effect** The lurker in light becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) until it enters an area of dim light or darkness, or until it uses a hostile action. **Lurker's Glow** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A creature that takes damage from a lurker in light's mote of light must attempt a DC 22 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to lurker's glow for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature is lined in golden light for 1 minute and can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) during this time. If the creature becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), it is concealed rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature sheds bright light in a 20-foot emanation for the duration of the effect. In addition, the creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) on its first turn after failing this save. **Ritual Gate** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Requirements** The lurker in light has reduced a living creature to 0 Hit Points on this turn or its previous turn and has a [_summon fey_](/Spells.aspx?ID=322) innate spell available; **Effect** The lurker in light casts _summon fey_ with only a verbal component, using the act of slaughter to replace the normal material and somatic components for the spell. If the fey creature summoned has the same alignment as the lurker in light, the lurker in light can sustain the _summon fey_ spell for up to 1 hour instead of 1 minute. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** A lurker in light's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
Greater Ritual Gates Well-versed and knowledgeable lurkers in light can lead groups of their kind in a more advanced Ritual Gate to enable travel to other planes, rather than just summoning. Doing so requires the sacrifice of a creature of equal or greater size to the lurker in light who wishes to travel the planes. Multiple lurkers in light can travel in this way, but this requires multiple sacrifices. The creation of a greater Ritual Gate is a 1-hour activity, at the end of which all affected lurkers in light travel to the same plane, chosen from the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), one of the Elemental Planes, or the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10).
name: Lurker In Light

A mandragora looks like a freshly pulled tuber that has grown into the malformed shape of a child with a grotesque face and hideously bloated body. These insidious little plants typically form when a mandrake root is watered with a [demon's](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) blood. Upon absorbing the otherworldly properties of the demon's blood, the root animates and is forced to seek out blood to feast from, lest it die of thirst. Always famished and in search for sustenance, mandragoras live haunted, pained lives and perform vile and desperate acts to acquire the blood they crave. While they prefer magically infused blood such as that of unicorns, fey, or sorcerers, and they can also feed off of potions, alchemical bombs, and other magical elixirs, a mandragora can subsist on the blood of non-magical creatures. They find the flavor of mundane blood to be bland and bitter, and they do not blanch at voicing these complaints to the creatures from which they drink. While the typical mandragora is the size of a human child, some of these evil plants continue to grow and grow, reaching sizes more comparable to those of giants. Sometimes as they grow, they form additional limbs or rudimentary faces, eventually transforming into truly hideous mockeries of the human form. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Mandragora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=727) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 170 **Perception** +11; blood scent, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Blood Scent** A mandragora can smell creatures with blood as an imprecise sense at a range of 30 feet, and it can smell [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), and [sorcerers](/Classes.aspx?ID=11) with blood as a precise sense at a range of 30 feet. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +13 **Will** +8 **HP** 60 **Resistances** bludgeoning 5, electricity 5 **Weaknesses** fire 5 **Vulnerability to Supernatural Darkness** Whenever a mandragora begins its turn in an area of magical darkness, it is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) on that turn. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 10 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** jaws +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d8+4 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** thorny vine +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d4+4 slashing plus mandragora venom **Blood Drain** **Requirements** The mandragora has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The mandragora drains blood from the creature it has grabbed, dealing 2d6 damage. If the creature is a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), or [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=11), the mandragora gains temporary Hit Points equal to the damage dealt. A creature that has its blood drained by a mandragora is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) until it receives healing of any kind or amount. **Mandragora Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 21 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage, [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), and stupefied 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison damage, confused, and stupefied 1 (1 round) **Piercing Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The mandragora emits an unsettling shriek. Each non-mandragora creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 25 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). As long as the creature remains sickened, this slowed condition value can't be reduced below 1.
Mandragora Sucklings Sometimes a mandragora offers its services to a spellcaster in exchange for sustenance. Tales tell of sorcerers or other magical creatures who keep mandragora “familiars” whose loyalty is sustained by feeding the little beasts with their own blood. These tales generally have gruesome endings wherein the mandragora is overcome with bloodlust and, unable to control itself, devours its master.
name: Mandragora

On the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), marrmoras dwell in ruined wildlands perpetually scourged by fire and rarely, if ever, travel elsewhere. When a wildfire devastates a wilderness region on the Material Plane and results in the death of other fey, marrmoras can be drawn across the planar boundary to revel in the resulting destruction. They seek to reignite the fires that beckoned them, to gather up and feed upon the charred remains of those who perished within (particularly the bodies of dead fey), though they do grow homesick if they spend too much time away from the First World. They are burdened by a capricious but persistent rage and are unfailingly cruel. While they are capable of negotiation and intelligent interaction, they almost never bargain in good faith and typically interact with others only as a means to more efficiently spread their fiery devastation. A marmorra's twisted appearance evokes the look of an [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8) whose bark has been burnt down to charcoal. They have nearly featureless faces and hands ending in long, sharp claws. Their broken flesh looks like charcoal-burnt wood, riddled with cracks that still glow with an unwholesome heat. They trail ash wherever they walk, and wisps of smoke curl off of their bodies. Though marrmoras enjoy the sight of any woodland and its inhabitants roasting in the flames of their carefully curated fires, there is little that brings more pleasure to a marrmora than the sight of good-aligned plant creatures cooking to a crisp. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Marrmora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=728) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 171 **Perception** +27; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +27 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +25 **Will** +27 **HP** 280 **Immunities** fire **Resistances** physical 10 (except slashing) **Weaknesses** cold iron 15 **Fascination of Flame** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 30 feet. A creature that enters or begins its turn in this aura's emanation must attempt a DC 33 Will save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature loses any resistance to fire for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature loses any resistance to fire for 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** The creature loses any resistance to fire for 1 hour and gains weakness 15 to fire for the same duration.
**Absorb Flame** **Trigger** The marrmora is targeted by a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) spell or effect, or is in the area of a fire effect; **Effect** The marrmora is healed by the fire damage, regaining Hit Points equal to half the damage the fire effect would have dealt. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** claw +29, **Damage** 3d6+14 slashing plus 3d6 fire and 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** flame jet +29 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 6d6 fire plus 2d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +28 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) - **6th** [Fire Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=118) (x3), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) (x3), [Tree Shape](/Spells.aspx?ID=342) (at will; appears as a burnt, dead tree) - **7th** [Elemental Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=98) ([fire elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=43) only), [Volcanic Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=360), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) - **8th** [Fire Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=118), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) **Igniting Assault** **Requirement** The marrmora is not under the effect of [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=118); **Effect** The marrmora makes a claw Strike. If it hits, it can immediately cast one of its available _fire shield_ innate spells as a free action.
Fey Manipulators Marrmoras exert a strange and subtle dominance over other fey. The fey under their control are filled with both horror at the destruction wrought by marrmoras and fascination with their fiery power.
name: Marrmora
