markdown:
Carnivorous blobs are the ravenous spawn of shattered worlds far beyond the stars, born across the galaxy in inert form until they fall like meteorites onto unsuspecting worlds. These massive beings can lie dormant for years in desolate caverns or barren wastelands. When a carnivorous blob perceives living creatures nearby, it lurches to gelatinous life, seeking out and consuming every creature it can catch until it is destroyed or until it has been unable to locate food for 24 hours, at which point it returns to hibernation. Often, keeping food away from a carnivorous blob is the safest way to defeat it. The blob's ability to split into smaller oozes that might stay hidden after a fight means it can be hard to fully eradicate these mindless predators. **[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Carnivorous Blob](/Monsters.aspx?ID=755) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 195 **Perception** +23; motion sense 240 feet, no vision **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** -3 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 **Motion Sense** A carnivorous blob can sense nearby creatures through vibration and air or water movement. --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +14 **Will** +19 **HP** 300 **Immunities** acid, critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), piercing, precision, slashing, sonic, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) **Split** When a carniverous blob that has 10 or more HP is hit by an attack that would deal piercing or slashing damage, it splits into two identical oozes, each with half the original's HP. One ooze is in the same space as the original, and the other is in an adjacent, unoccupied space. If no adjacent space is unoccupied, it automatically pushes creatures and objects out of the way to fill a space (the GM decides if an object or creature is too big or heavy to push).
**Reactive Strikes** **Trigger** The carnivorous blob takes damage from any source; **Effect** The blob makes a pseudopod Strike against an adjacent target. If an adjacent creature dealt the triggering damage, that creature is the target of this Reactive Strike. --- **Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** pseudopod +26 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+12 bludgeoning plus 2d6 acid and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Carnivorous Blob Acid** A carnivorous blob's acid damages only flesh—not bone, stone, wood, or other materials— but is nonetheless devastating. Whenever a creature takes damage from this acid, it must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 2 on a critical failure). On each subsequent failure, the drained condition value increases by 1 (or by 2 on a critical failure), to a maximum of drained 4. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d12 bludgeoning plus 2d6 acid, DC 33 **[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14)** DC 33, 4d10 acid, Escape DC 33, Rupture 20
name: Carnivorous Blob

markdown:
Peludas are wild and savage dragons that favor reedy marshlands, sparsely forested bogs, and river valleys for lairs. A single peluda typically claims a stretch of waterways a few miles in length, then systematically drives off any potential rivals so that the peluda can despoil the land as they please, leaving clawed footprints on muddy riverbanks, toppling trees, and impaling half-eaten corpses on their quills as trophies left to rot. They venture beyond their domains to hunt, roasting prey with their breath or pulverizing them with their tails, burning and killing for cruel sport before slithering, sated, back to their mucky lairs. Peludas despise weakness and loathe creatures smaller than themselves, especially humanoids, whom they see as fragile and soft, good only for eating or breaking. In contrast, peludas have a massive inferiority complex regarding more impressive creatures—especially larger and stronger dragons. Peludas will try anything to become stronger, including ingesting shiny coins, gems, and jewelry, which old folk tales claim will strengthen their quills and fire breath. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Peluda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=756) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 196 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +17 **Will** +19 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 170 **Immunities** fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Vulnerable Tail** If the peluda takes 30 or more slashing damage from a critical hit, the attacker severs the peluda's tail. The peluda takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and can't make tail attacks until their tail grows back (in about 1 week).
**Quill Thrust** **Trigger** A creature within 10 feet attempts a melee Strike against the peluda; **Effect** The peluda shifts their position and makes a quill Strike against the attacking creature. This Strike doesn't count toward the peluda's multiple attack penalty, and the peluda's multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike. --- **Speed** 30 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +23 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+13 piercing **Melee** quill +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6 piercing plus peluda venom **Melee** tail +23 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile piercing](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d6+13 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Breath Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The peluda breathes a torrent of flames that deals 7d10 fire damage in a 60-foot line (DC 29 basic Reflex save). They can't use their Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Peluda Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison and [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison, [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), and flat-footed (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison, enfeebled 2, and flat-footed (1 round) **Quill Barrage** The peluda bristles their quills and shakes, sending dozens of spear-like barbs in every direction. All creatures within 30 feet take 11d6 piercing damage (DC 29 basic Reflex save) and are exposed to peluda venom if they take any damage. The peluda can't use Quill Barrage again for 1 minute.
Peluda Patrons Occasionally a particularly cunning band of humanoids—typically [lizardfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=71) or [orcs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=80)—finds a way to assuage a local peluda's temper and then elevate the dragon to the status of a local liege, patron, or even object of worship. Although peludas rarely have any actual affection for such servants, they revel in the submission of these weaker creatures and the gifts of gold and food they bring.
name: Peluda

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Peryton](/Monsters.aspx?ID=757)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The peryton is an amalgam of horror, merging the features of a stag, wolf, and hawk. They love using shadows to stalk their victims before ripping out their still-beating hearts and relishing the dying pulse as they swallow them whole, so the doomed victims spends their last moments watching as their flesh is consumed.

 Despite perytons' animalistic appearance and brutal behavior, they are viciously cunning and as intelligent as humans. Their preferred tactic is to find a lone traveler and stalk it from on high. Most perytons are loners who loathe all other creatures. These feelings extend to other perytons as well, and adult males attack each other on sight as surely as they attack anything else.

 Once per year, perytons mate. The males offer up hearts they have collected from humanoids to attract females. Those that manage to secure a mate rarely survive long though, as their mating ritual is short and violent. Female perytons generally abandon their eggs after laying them, so newly hatched perytons must fend for themselves from the moment they emerge.

 A lone peryton typically builds a nest in a rocky overhang or an exceptionally tall and strong tree where they can observe nearby lands. Although they aren't particularly interested in wealth, perytons do like to collect small objects from their victims. During those times between hunts, they sometimes study these trophies in order to relive the memories of victims' last moments and the taste of victims' hearts.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Peryton.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Peryton](/Monsters.aspx?ID=757)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 197

**Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
antler +14 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174)),
**Damage** 1d12+7 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d8+7 slashing

**Heart Ripper** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The peryton rips out the heart of an adjacent corpse with their jaws. The creature must have died in the last minute. As the peryton rips the heart free and swallows it whole, they regain 2d6 HP, and any non-peryton that witnesses this event must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (or frightened 2 on a critical failure).

**Mimic Shadow** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143)) **Requirements** The target must be casting a shadow; **Effect** The peryton Flies, going no higher than 20 feet over the target creature. The target creature must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or the peryton's shadow changes to match that cast by the target creature. With their shadow so transformed, the peryton gains a +2 status bonus to attack and damage rolls against that creature. In addition, each time the peryton successfully Strikes that creature, the creature must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or increase its frightened condition by 1 if it's already frightened. This is an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect. The shadow remains transformed for 1 hour or until the peryton Mimics a Shadow again, whichever comes first.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Peryton Flocks</title>

On rare occasions, a peryton flock forms at the lead of an alpha female. It lasts until the alpha makes a mistake that erodes her perceived power. Then the rest of the flock tears her apart.
</aside>
name: Peryton

markdown:
When a mortal dies, their soul travels to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) in the Outer Planes where they are judged by [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14), the goddess of the dead. Once they have been judged, their soul is sent on to their final reward or punishment in the afterlife, and in the process is transformed into a creature known as a petitioner. This process grants the soul a new body, one whose shape is the result of the prevailing philosophical forces of the plane to which it is sent. The petitioner's memories from their life are typically wiped nearly clean, allowing them to retain only a few hazy fragments akin to half-remembered dreams. Regardless of the petitioner's size, power, or nature in life, they're a Medium creature in their afterlife. Existence as a petitioner can last for eons, but this state is not necessarily eternal. Deities, powerful denizens of the Great Beyond, or even the outer planes themselves can further change the petitioner's nature by transforming them into raw quintessence, spiritual essence that is then used to expand a plane's physical manifestation, or by transforming the petitioner into a new form of supernatural life such as a [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), or [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70). Should a petitioner die, their body breaks down in a process akin to decay, their nature reverting back to the quintessence or elements that make up their plane. This represents the true end of a soul's journey: their associated life essence travels back to the heart of the Great Beyond to be recycled into the [Positive Energy plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=3), fueling the creation of entirely new souls. **[Recall Knowledge - Petitioner](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Petitioner](/Monsters.aspx?ID=758) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 198 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** see Planar Incarnation **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Planar Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +7 **Str** +2 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +7 **Will** +7 **HP** 22 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Planar Incarnation** All petitioners are formed from and personify the nature of the plane on which they manifest, and their statistics are adjusted as summarized below. They also gain any trait associated with creatures from their plane.
**[Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12) (the Hunted)** The hunted appear as Medium sized versions of themselves from their prior lives, and they exist to be pursued and consumed by daemons; **Alignment** NE; **Language** [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); **Additional Ability** [fast healing 5](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15); **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) (the Larvae)** The larvae appear as maggot-like grubs with the face the petitioners had in life; **Alignment** CE; **Language** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); **Additional Ability** immune to [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126); **Melee** jaws +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing
**[Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8) (the Untethered)** The untethered appear as astrally projected versions of their mortal forms, yet they lack a silver cord; **Alignment** any; **Language** any one spoken in life (such as [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)); **Additional Ability** fly Speed 20 feet; **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Axis](/Planes.aspx?ID=14) (the Unmade)** The unmade appear as parchment-skinned humanoids covered in lines of close black script; **Alignment** LN; **Language** [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36); **Additional Ability** immune to [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68); **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) (the Dead)** The dead appear as animated skeletons of the type of creature they were when they were alive; **Alignment** N; **Language** [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); **Additional Ability** resist piercing and slashing 3; Melee claw +7, Damage 1d8+2 slashing
**Dead Vault (the Caged)** The caged are sent to [Rovagug's](/Deities.aspx?ID=15) prison plane, the Dead Vault; they appear as chained versions of their mortal bodies, save that their faces are hideous insectile visages; **Alignment** CE; **Language** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); **Additional Ability** [Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30) bite; **Melee** bite +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing
**[Dimension of Dreams](/Traits.aspx?ID=299) (the Dreamers)** The dreamers appear as their ideal appearance, age, and gender from their mortal bodies; **Alignment** any; **Language** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); **Additional Ability** +1 circumstance bonus on all saving throws; **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=16) (the Chosen)** The chosen appear as idealized versions of their mortal selves with glowing eyes; **Alignment** CG; **Language** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16); **Additional Ability** immune to [_blindness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=26) and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7); **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9) (the Terrorized)** The terrorized appear as sickly, haunted-looking versions of their mortal selves; **Alignment** any; **Language** any one spoken in life (such as [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)); **Additional Ability** immune to bleed and [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects; **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=17) (the Elect)** The elect have golden halos and ghostly wings, but they otherwise appear as their mortal forms; **Alignment** LG; **Language** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16); **Additional Ability** fly Speed 20 feet; **Melee** wing +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18) (the Damned)** The damned look like terribly scarred or mutilated versions of their mortal selves; **Alignment** LE; **Language** [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); **Additional Ability** immune to fire; **Melee** jaws +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing
**[Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19) (the Shapeless)** The shapeless appear as half-melted reflections of their former mortal selves; **Alignment** CN; **Language** [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); **Additional Ability** immune to critical hits and precision; **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**Material Plane (the Remnants)** The remnants are among the rarest of petitioners; they appear as plain, bland versions of the ancestry of their mortal lives; **Alignment** any; **Language** any one spoken in life (such as [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)); **Additional Ability** [ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16); **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=20) (the Cleansed)** The cleansed take on animal forms that match their personalities in life; **Alignment** NG; **Language** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16); **Additional Ability** speed 35 feet; **Melee** claw +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 slashing
**[Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4) (the Air Pneuma)** Air pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves composed of air; **Alignment** any; **Language** [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15); **Additional Ability** fly Speed 20 feet; **Melee** gust +7 (air), **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) (the Earth Pneuma)** Earth pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves built of earth or stone; **Alignment** any; **Language** [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22); **Additional Ability** burrow Speed 20 feet; **Melee** fist +7 (earth), **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6) (the Fire Pneuma)** Fire pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves shaped from flame; **Alignment** any; **Language** [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18); **Additional Ability** immunity to fire; **Melee** cinder +7 (fire), **Damage** 1d8+2 fire
**[Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7) (the Water Pneuma)** Water pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves formed of flowing water; **Alignment** any; **Language** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14); **Additional Ability** [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207), swim Speed 20 feet; **Melee** current +7 (water), **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Positive Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=3) (the Enlightened)** The enlightened appear as diaphanous, radiant versions of their mortal selves; **Alignment** any good; **Language** [Jyoti](/Languages.aspx?ID=60); **Additional Ability** [fast healing 5](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15); **Melee** glowing touch +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 positive
**[Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) (the Mutilated)** The mutilated appear as they did in their mortal lives, yet are covered in wounds or partially adorned or wrapped in chains; **Alignment** any evil; **Language** [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21); **Additional Ability** immune to bleed and [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68); **Melee** chain +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
First World Petitioners A soul sent to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) does not become a petitioner. Rather, they immediately reincarnate into a [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) creature whose temperament and role match their mortal personalities. This process tends to leave souls who incarnate into the First World with slightly more fragments of memories than most petitioners, yet still not enough that they remember who they once were.
Other Petitioners Each plane (save the [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2)) has its own category of petitioner, but not every plane or demiplane present in the Pathfinder setting is listed here. Certainly, planes of your own design aren't represented! You can use the wide range of petitioner types and descriptions here as templates or inspiration for creating new types of petitioners: each needs a name, appearance, alignment, language, additional ability, and attack. For instance, [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5) maintains a demiplane called Cynosure that straddles the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) and [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=16). Petitioners on Cynosure are known as the chosen dreamers, and they appear as idealized and softly glowing versions of their mortal selves; they are chaotic good, speak [Celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), are immune to [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=48) and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), and deal bludgeoning damage with their fists.
name: Petitioner

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ifrit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=759)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ifrits' blood blazes with elemental fire. Most ifrits are second-class citizens who serve under the iron heel of the [efreeti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=214) and their Dominion of Flame, but those born outside the efreet hierarchy or who choose to flee it live lives of passion in search of fame, glory, and power.

 Ifrits are fond of fire, but this doesn't mean they all embrace fire's destructive nature. Those who seek more productive roles in a society find inspiration in the way flames flit and dance, and they pride themselves in their skills as acrobats or dancers. The church of Sarenrae particularly welcomes ifrit fire dancers, both in appreciation of their skill and to help ensure these ifrits have a safe place apart from their more violent kin.

 But by and large, ifrits are drawn to professions and callings that allow them to wallow in the glories of fire. Ifrit pyrochemists apply this calling to alchemical teachings, seeing purity in every single bomb thrown or conflagration lit.

markdown:
Elemental earth laces through the bones of oreads, who appear similar to stone statues of their mortal ancestry, with delicate crystals in place of hair, fur, or scales. Oreads are stoic and slow to plan but are of steadfast resolve and unwavering in their convictions. The typical oread cherishes quiet seclusion. Yet as they age, many oreads find themselves inexplicably drawn to some far-flung location with a pull like that exerted on the needle of a compass—intangible, constant, and ultimately irresistible. The destination of this mysterious pilgrimage is unique to each oread, though it usually ends in some place of great mystical power, natural splendor, or esoteric learning. Most oreads are drawn to a place with which they are somewhat familiar, but a rare few feel drawn to travel in a seemingly random direction, departing with only their hope that they'll discover whatever mystery lies at the end of their invisible path. Many oreads find that the role of a guard suits their personality well, for in such a role they can feel as if they are helping to promote order but also find time to stand vigil as lone sentinels over a specific portion of a fortification or a remote location on a wilderness trail. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Oread Guard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=760) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 201 **Perception** +7 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +3, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +5, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +3, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +5 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** [Bastard Sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=15), [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13), [light hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=29), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 19 (21 with shield raised) **Fort** +7 **Ref** +6 **Will** +5 **HP** 20 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** bastard sword +9 ([two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 1d8+4 slashing **Ranged** light hammer +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning **Power Attack** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The guard makes a melee Strike. This counts as two attacks when calculating the guard's multiple attack penalty. If this Strike hits, the guard deals an extra die of weapon damage.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Suli](/Monsters.aspx?ID=761)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Because their genie forebears are native to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), sulis (scions of mortals and [jann](/Monsters.aspx?ID=211)) are by and large the most common geniekin on the Material Plane. They are often artisans and peace brokers, compelled to try and bring harmony and balance in a world wrought with discord.

 Sulis have a natural charm that often eludes other geniekin, but tend to layer on a level of boastful pride or even arrogance as a personal quirk or humorous facade. Few non-sulis realize that bragging is not simply the symptom of a puffed-up ego for sulis, but instead is a cultural institution easily comparable to human poetry. Sulis' boasts not only glorify themselves, but also secure their companions' and families' accomplishments in history, with the ultimate goal of spinning stories that will be retold for generations. This is especially true for sulis who have lived with other geniekin and have been seen as lesser for not having a strong connection to an Elemental Plane.

 Suli dune dancers are but one way these geniekin seek to integrate with other humanoid societies. They work to hone their skills at boasting to an extent that their claims help to bolster those they travel with. Suli from regions other than deserts adjust their names to match their terrains, but regardless of whether they prefer forests, hills, or arctic plains, their boasts remain as compelling.

**Elemental Bulwark** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An enemy is about to damage the dune dancer with cold, electricity, or fire, or with a spell that has the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait; **Effect** The dune dancer gain resistance 2 against the triggering damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
scimitar +7 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 slashing

**Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 17, attack +9
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Inspire Courage](/Spells.aspx?ID=386), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **1st**
[Color Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=44), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=291), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) (2 slots)

**Elemental Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Elemental magic fills the dune dancer's body or weapon. The dune dancer chooses one element and makes a melee Strike. The Strike deals an additional 1d4 damage of the indicated type and has the trait corresponding to the element: **Air** electricity, **Earth** bludgeoning, **Fire** fire, or **Water** cold.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sylph](/Monsters.aspx?ID=762)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Born with elemental gales coursing through their breath, sylphs are wispy planar scions whose bodies seem caught in a perpetual, gentle breeze. Born of unions between mortals and [djinn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=212), sylphs are quick-witted and creative, but they are prone to flights of fancy and tend to be easily distracted.

 Sylphs are notorious for their practice of “listening to the wind,” which most others dismiss as a fancy name for eavesdropping. Yet this custom means much more to sylphs, who spend hours listening to the stories brought to them on the proverbial breeze. While some less scrupulous sylphs may use the information they learn to blackmail or abuse others, most of them see listening to the wind as their way of staying connected to the world around them while still keeping it comfortably at arm's length. Certainly the typical sylph sneak doesn't seek to use what they learn for ill, but instead tries to warn others of dangers yet unrealized or to prepare themself for a dangerous task.

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Undine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=763)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Undines are infused with elemental water—the churning power of the briny deep flows through them. These planar scions are often athletic and lithe, but they are easily distracted by auditory sensations because of how much louder and clearer sound rings above the waves.

 Undines are perhaps the most settled of all geniekin, often forming communities along the coast or even on the water itself. In the latter case, they prefer to settle in ship-towns that sometimes number dozens of vessels in all shapes and sizes. The undines who fill these communities are similarly diverse, bringing aspects of multiple cultures together to form one whole. Ship-towns are permanent, but fluid—they grow, recede, and migrate constantly as undine families add their ships to or remove them from the flotilla. An undine town like this may remain at sea for years, its residents coming ashore only on rare occasions to collect wood for cookfires or to repair their homes. The paradoxically ever-shifting permanence of these communities reflects the undine virtues of adaptability and freedom, while also maintaining the prime importance of the community.

 Undine hydromancers are quite valued on these floating settlements, if only for their ability to create fresh drinking water while adrift on the sea.

Akizendris gnaw at sources of knowledge and lore, gleefully corrupting and altering them to vex scholars and sages across the planes with contradictions and untruths. These proteans appear as cackling, serpentine creatures with elongated crocodilian faces, luminous eyes, and arms that vanish at the elbows into swirling clouds of runes before reforming into solid talons. Akizendris amuse themselves by rearranging or deleting texts, placing insulting acrostics in poetic verses, and penning crude insults upon title pages. Libraries in such places as [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=17), [Axis](/Planes.aspx?ID=14), and [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18) are especially keen to avoid such contamination, knowing that their records are choice targets for every akizendri in existence. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Akizendri](/Monsters.aspx?ID=764) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 204 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), entropy sense (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); [telepathy (touch)](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +11, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +9 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Entropy Sense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=132)) An akizendri can anticipate the most likely location of a creature through their supernatural insight into the forces of chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants the akizendri the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. A creature under the effects of [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209) or that is otherwise shielded from [divinations](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) and [predictions](/Traits.aspx?ID=132) cannot be noticed via entropy sense. --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +11 **Will** +10 **HP** 42 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 1) **Resistances** precision 3, protean anatomy 6 **Weaknesses** lawful 3 **Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) An akizendri's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the akizendri takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, it gains the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case its resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The akizendri is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effects unless it is a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), the akizendri automatically recovers at the end of its next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet, swim 25 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +12 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+3 piercing plus 1d4 chaotic and garbled thoughts **Melee** tail +12 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+3 bludgeoning plus 1d4 chaotic and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 20, attack +12 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will, lawful only) - **3rd** [Glyph of Warding](/Spells.aspx?ID=138), [Secret Page](/Spells.aspx?ID=270) - **Constant (4th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The akizendri takes on the appearance of any Small or smaller creature. This doesn't change its Speed or its attack and damage bonuses with its Strikes, but might change the damage type its Strikes deal. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d8+3 bludgeoning, DC 20 **Garbled Thoughts** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) A creature hit by the akizendri's bite Strike must attempt a DC 20 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round. **Text Immersion** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When the akizendri casts [_secret page_](/Spells.aspx?ID=270), it can physically immerse itself in the text, changing the message of the text in the process. It can exit the book at any point by Dismissing _secret page_, at which point it appears in a space adjacent to the text. If it does so to begin combat, it rolls a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check for initiative. As long as it remains immersed in the text, the akizendri has no body. It can communicate telepathically with a creature as long as the creature touches the book or scroll that contains the secret page. It can sense nearby creatures using its entropy sense, but not in any other way, nor can it use any attack, manipulate, or move actions or speak aloud. If the object it is immersed in is destroyed, the akizendri reappears in an adjacent square and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
Vandals of Chaos Those who seek secret knowledge beyond their means to purchase can bargain with akizendris to provide them with rare tomes in exchange for delivering the immersed akizendri into a library via another book. From there, the akizendri can vandalize books to its heart's content without being detected.

Azuretzis are sinuous, serpentine creatures with dagger-sharp teeth, covered in brilliant blue scales with mottled purple and pink highlights that shimmer in a pareidolic approximation of leering, laughing faces. The [Maelstrom's](/Planes.aspx?ID=19) chaotic forces spawn these small proteans from a variety of sources: physical mating between older azuretzis, the paradoxical promotion of bestial [naunets](/Monsters.aspx?ID=337), and possibly from mortal [petitioners](/Monsters.aspx?ID=758), though azuretzis may just be confusing putative mortal memories with experiences gained from games of mimicry. Never expect azuretzis to operate by any rational, self-consistent rules. Azuretzis represent the humor of chaos, particularly in the form of mockery and parody via exaggerated mimicry, twisting a target's features into a laughingstock. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Azuretzi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=765) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 205 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), entropy sense (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +13 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Entropy Sense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=132)) An azuretzi can anticipate the most likely location of a creature through their supernatural insight into the forces of chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants the azuretzi the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. A creature under the effects of [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209) or that is otherwise shielded from [divinations](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) and [predictions](/Traits.aspx?ID=132) cannot be noticed via entropy sense. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +15 **Will** +11 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 75 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 2) **Resistances** precision 5, protean anatomy 8 **Weaknesses** lawful 5 **Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) An azuretzi's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the azuretzi takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, it gains the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case its resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The azuretzi is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effects unless it is a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), the azuretzi automatically recovers at the end of its next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones.
**Spell Pilfer** **Trigger** A creature with an active spell effect within 30 feet of the azuretzi fails to resist another azuretzi's Mocking Touch; **Effect** The azuretzi attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) one ongoing spell effect on the target creature with a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check. If it is successful, it transfers the ongoing spell effect to itself. The creature then becomes temporarily immune to Spell Pilfer for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet, swim 25 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +15 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d10+4 piercing plus 1d6 chaotic **Melee** claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+4 slashing plus 1d6 chaotic **Melee** tail +13 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+4 bludgeoning plus 1d6 chaotic and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 22 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; lawful only) - **2nd** [Hideous Laughter](/Spells.aspx?ID=150) - **3rd** [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **Constant (4th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d12+4 bludgeoning, DC 22 **Mimic Form** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) As [Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8) but an azuretzi can assume the specific form of a Medium or smaller creature it Seeks with a successful DC 25 Perception check. It can return to its true form as a free action. It can retain access to only one exact appearance at a time. **Mocking Touch** ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) **Requirement** The azuretzi is not currently using Mocking Touch on a spell; **Effect** The azuretzi mocks a creature's magical ability with a touch. The azuretzi must attempt a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check against the target's Will DC.
**Critical Success** The azuretzi chooses one spell of up to 3rd level that the target creature has available to cast. The azuretzi gains that spell as a mock divine innate spell, and it can cast that spell once as an innate divine spell using its own DC and spell attack. It must do so before 24 hours pass or the mock spell is lost. The creature touched cannot cast the mock spell until the azuretzi casts it first or the 24 hour period passes, whichever comes first.
**Success** As critical success, but the azuretzi has 1 hour to cast the mock spell before it is lost, and the creature touched can cast the spell normally.
**Failure** As critical success, but the azuretzi must cast the mock spell before the end of its next turn or it is lost, and the creature touched can cast the spell normally.
**Critical Failure** Mocking Touch has no effect.
Mock Choruses Azuretzis form mock choruses in emulation of their more powerful kindred, aping the shifting philosophies of proper choruses. While mischievous or outright malevolent to hostile or lawful creatures, they interact amiably with chaotic creatures, who run the risk of being promptly followed–willingly or not–by a serpentine gaggle of self-appointed understudies.
name: Azuretzi

The loquacious proteans known as imenteshes serve as missionaries, spies, and heralds of chaos to further the protean goal of reality's dissolution. Imenteshes are cunning, curiously diplomatic, and profoundly whimsical. They travel beyond the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19) for their work, frequently adopting extravagant personal attire and decorations that often border upon the garish, all to curry favor with less chaotic beings. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Imentesh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=766) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 207 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), entropy sense (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +19, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +17 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** +7 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Entropy Sense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=132)) An imentesh can anticipate the most likely location of a creature through their supernatural insight into the forces of chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants the imentesh the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. A creature under the effects of [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209) or that is otherwise shielded from [divinations](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) and [predictions](/Traits.aspx?ID=132) cannot be noticed via entropy sense. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 175 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 5) **Resistances** precision 10, protean anatomy 15 **Weaknesses** lawful 10 **Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) An imentesh's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the imentesh takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, it gains the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case its resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The imentesh is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effects unless it is a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), the imentesh automatically recovers at the end of its next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet, swim 25 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +23 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus 1d6 chaotic and warpwave strike **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+11 slashing plus 1d6 chaotic **Melee** tail +23 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 chaotic and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; lawful only) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=189), [Shrink Item](/Spells.aspx?ID=285), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) - **4th** [Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=54), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (chaotic), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) - **Constant (4th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The imentesh takes the appearance of any Large or smaller creature. This doesn't change its Speed or its attack and damage bonuses with its Strikes, but might change the damage type its Strikes deal. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d10+11 bludgeoning, DC 29 **Inflict Warpwave** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) An imentesh inflicts a warpwave on a creature within 100 feet (see Warpwave below; DC 29 Fortitude save to resist). **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** An imentesh's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) targets. **Warpwave Strike** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Any creature struck and damaged by an imentesh's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or be subject to a warpwave.
Mouthpieces of Chaos While rarely found on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), imenteshes frequent interplanar hubs of culture and commerce. Imenteshes act as diplomats on behalf of any chorus they claim allegiance to. They can offer safe travel through the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19) or even act as guides through the Maelstrom's ever-shifting Borderlands.
name: Imentesh

Esoboks are brute hunters and pugnacious sentinels that serve as the guard dogs of the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15). These squat, powerful quadrupeds have a frill of dark feathers around their distinctive heads which resemble a crocodile skull. Esoboks rarely bother with those who are truly dead, allowing the dead of the Boneyard to go about their business while remaining watchful for danger. Though cunning when sniffing out threats to the Boneyard or to their psychopomp handlers, they're among the least intelligent of the psychopomps and rarely speak except to utter growling threats. The wise listen when an esobok makes a threat, as it won't do so twice. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Esobok](/Monsters.aspx?ID=767) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 208 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), [scent (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +9, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +10 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +8 **Will** +8 **HP** 55 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) **Resistances** negative 5, poison 5 --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +12 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d10+3 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) and spirit touch **Melee** claw +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d6+3 slashing plus spirit touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 17 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (×3; self only) **Pounce** The esobok Strides and then makes a Strike. If it began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), it remains hidden until after the Strike. **Spirit Touch** An esobok's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deal 1d6 negative damage to living creatures and 1d6 positive damage to undead. **Wrench Spirit** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirement** A creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the esobok's jaws; **Effect** The esobok releases the target from the Grab but wrenches its spirit free as it does so. The creature must attempt a DC 20 Will save. Creatures without souls (such as most [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=35)) and creatures whose bodies and souls are one (such as most [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70), and [monitors](/Traits.aspx?ID=303)) that roll a failure or critical failure on the save get a success instead.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The esobok wrenches the target's soul from its body into its jaws. [Mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures of level 2 or lower are destroyed, other undead creatures are stunned for 1 round, and all other creatures are [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). At the end of each of its turns, a creature paralyzed by this effect can attempt a new save to end the effect. The paralysis ends automatically if the esobok attempts a jaws Strike or speaks.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but as long as a creature is stunned or paralyzed, it is also [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37).
name: Esobok

Not all spirits who enter the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) realize they have died. Catrinas meet these souls, helping to convince them of the finality of their fate to ease a spirit's passing. Catrinas are more likely to intervene when a mortal can't accept their death. They perform their task to keep the afterlife calm, rather than out of true compassion for a mortal's grief. Catrinas only rarely visit the Material Plane, typically to help an extremely important mortal pass on. Catrinas resemble skeletons dressed in bright flowers and colorful dresses, giving them a simultaneously festive and macabre appearance. Though most catrinas present as feminine, masculine catrinas still dress in bright colors and carry garlands of flowers. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Catrina](/Monsters.aspx?ID=768) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 209 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [telepathy 120 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37), [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +12, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +12 **Str** +0 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +12 **Will** +13 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 75 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) **Resistances** negative 5, poison 5 **Calming Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) 30 feet. A
creature that begins its turn within the area must attempt a DC 18 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to calming presence for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature's attack rolls take a –1 status penalty for 1 round.
**Failure** Any [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects that would affect the creature are suppressed and the creature can't use hostile actions. If the creature is subjected to hostility from any other creature, it ceases to be affected by calming presence and is temporarily immune to calming presence for 24 hours.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but hostility doesn't end the effect. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d8+2 bludgeoning plus spirit touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 22 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only) - **3rd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=329) (at will) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Compel Condemned** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The catrina telepathically compels a creature within 30 feet to approach and allow the catrina to kiss them, in preparation for using Kiss of Death. The target must attempt a DC 22 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Compel Condemned for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature must spend each of its actions to move closer to the catrina as quickly as possible, while avoiding obvious dangers. If the compelled creature is adjacent to the catrina, it stays still and doesn't act. If the creature takes any damage, the effect ends and the creature is temporarily immune to Compel Condemned for 24 hours. This effect lasts for 1 round, but if the catrina uses this ability again on subsequent rounds, it extend the duration by 1 round for all affected creatures.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but damage does not end the effect. **Kiss of Death** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The catrina gives a long, passionate kiss to an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) or willing creature, dealing 3d6 negative damage. Any creature damaged by the same catrina's Kiss of Death for 3 consecutive rounds becomes unconscious and is [dying 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11). **Spirit Touch** An catrina's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deal 1d6 negative damage to living creatures and 1d6 positive damage to undead.

Protecting the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) are the stern and resolute guardians of the dead known as vanths, psychopomps who resemble skeletons with raven-like wings and a mask resembling a vulture's skull. Vanths carry black scythes to fight against those who would disturb the natural progression of souls, and they consider any visitor to the Boneyard a potential troublemaker. When the psychopomp armies go to war, vanths serve as front-line soldiers. They rarely speak and even more rarely show any emotion other than a grim adherence to duty. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Vanth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=769) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 210 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +13, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Items** [_+1 scythe_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 105 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) **Resistances** negative 10, poison 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 20 feet, DC 22
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** scythe +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d10+8 slashing plus spirit touch **Melee** jaws +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d6+8 slashing plus spirit touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 22 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only) - **3rd** [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173) (×3), [Searing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=269) (×3) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) **Infuse Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) A vanth's scythe is its symbol of office and gains a measure of its personal power. This scythe becomes a _+1 scythe_ and is treated as if it were [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) while the vanth wields it. A vanth whose scythe is taken or destroyed can infuse a new one with an hour of work. **Spirit Touch** An vanth's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deal 2d6 negative damage to living creatures and 2d6 positive damage to undead. **Vanth's Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The vanth bestows a curse on a creature by touching it with its scythe. The creature must attempt a DC 25 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Vanth's Curse for 24 hours.
**Success** The target feels a momentary shudder of doom and is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute by the distracting sensation.
**Failure** The target becomes morose and glum as it accepts its own inevitable fate. For 1 hour, the target is stupefied 2. Each time the target gains the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) condition, the stupefied condition value increases by 1, to a maximum value of stupefied 4.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect is permanent.
Vanth Scythes Vanths' favored weapon is the [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39), a choice that even further adds to their fearsome appearance and can lead to unfortunate associations with the [Grim Reaper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=250) or [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19) among more superstitious mortals. Vanths find such comparisons to be ignorant at best or insulting at worst. Some vanths infuse their scythes with different qualities, such as [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379), depending on the nature of their most common enemies.
name: Vanth

The greatest judges among the psychopomps are the massive yamarajes, whose wisdom is legendary and whose edicts are unappealable except to ushers or [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) herself. A yamaraj resembles an immense dragon with dark, feathery scales and an emotionless, dispassionate gaze behind a feathered mask. When not serving as the senior magistrates, lords, and generals of the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15), yamarajes pursue highly individualistic hobbies, such as gardening or literature. They are willing to halt in their duties to converse with mortals whose expertise matches their interests. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Yamaraj](/Monsters.aspx?ID=770) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 211 **Perception** +37; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense 240 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [telepathy 120 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37), [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +36, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +40, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +36, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +40, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +38, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +38, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +38 **Str** +10 **Dex** +7 **Con** +7 **Int** +10 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +31 **Will** +35 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 375 ([fast healing 20](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15), lightning drinker) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), electricity (see lightning drinker) **Resistances** negative 20, poison 20 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet, DC 39
**Lightning Drinker** Whenever a yamaraj would take electricity damage if not for its immunity, its fast healing increases to 40 on its next turn. During that turn, if it uses its breath weapon, the beetles crackle with electricity and deal 2d12 additional electricity damage. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 50 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) and yamaraj venom and spirit touch **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d4+18 slashing plus spirit touch **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+18 bludgeoning plus spirit touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 44 - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193) (at will) - **8th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33) (×3), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (×3), [Spirit Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=303), [Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=363) - **9th** [Bind Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=21), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Wail of the Banshee](/Spells.aspx?ID=361) - **10th** [Miracle](/Spells.aspx?ID=196), [Revival](/Spells.aspx?ID=262) - **Constant (10th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Rituals** DC 44 - **5th** [Call Spirit](/Rituals.aspx?ID=5), [Resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) **Breath Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The yamaraj breathes a blast of beetles in a 50-foot cone that deals 14d8 slashing damage and 4d8 [persistent slashing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to creatures in the area (DC 42 Reflex save). It can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is sickened 3. **Final Judgment** A yamaraj's miracle spells are used only to pronounce judgment, typically either to restore a dead or destroyed creature to life, bind a creature to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15), or banish a creature from the Boneyard. **Spirit Touch** A yamaraj's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deal 3d6 negative damage to living creatures and 3d6 positive damage to undead. **Yamaraj Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) While a creature is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) from this poison, it is [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) with the same value; **Saving Throw** DC 42 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d8 poison damage and clumsy 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 5d8 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 7d8 poison damage and clumsy 3 (1 round)
name: Yamaraj

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dig-Widget](/Monsters.aspx?ID=771)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Thieves covet dig-widgets, specialized constructs built for infiltration. Each dig-widget contains numerous simple tools, including a set of mechanical devices that function as [thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58), two arms with drills, and two arms with corkscrews for attaching to and climbing surfaces. Once activated, these devices propel themselves forward. Though they have the full faculties typical of a construct, they usually follow a simple routine: avoid notice, pick any lock barring the path, dig past obstacles, and attack if caught. They're rarely left unattended, as a thief needs to be nearby to follow after-both to steal goods and to stop the dig-widget from engaging in further larceny once it's achieved its goal.

 The source of a dig-widget's power is as much mechanical as it is magical. The gears and springs that provide a dig-widget mobility are an improvement over more primitive true clockwork creations (whose functions require constant winding to remain mobile), but at the cost of security, for a dig-widget's moving parts can be dismantled quickly by thieves and others with the proper training.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/DigWidget.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Dig-Widget](/Monsters.aspx?ID=771)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=35" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 79

**Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +5

**Con** +1

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

**Infiltration Tools** A dig-widget's face consists of a set of [infiltrator thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58). They can be salvaged from a destroyed dig-widget with a successful DC 20 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check. On a failed check, the tools are destroyed.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 65

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), nonlethal attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Mechanical Vulnerability** A creature with expert proficiency in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) can attempt a check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) to damage a dig-widget. The DC is 22, and each success deals 20 damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, burrow 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
drill +14 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d6+4 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
corkscrew +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 piercing

**Fastening Leap** <actions string="Single Action" />  The dig-widget Leaps up 20 feet onto a creature or object and attempts a corkscrew Strike against it. If the Strike damages the target, the dig-widget attaches to the target (typically to the back of a creature). This is similar to [Grabbing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the creature, but the dig-widget moves with that creature rather than holding it in place. While attached, the dig-widget can't use its corkscrew. The dig-widget can be [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) off, or it can detach itself with an Interact action.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   A dig-widget's Strikes deal an additional 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Widget Workshops</title>

The first dig-widget came from the workshops of a [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) thieves' guild called Godak's Grifters, which used more advanced magical clockwork theories as a springboard for these simpler but no less effective contraptions. With their dig-widgets, the Grifters plagued the authorities of several dwarven settlements over the years. Their travels spread dig-widget technology, and numerous improvements have since led to faster and more reliable versions. Though upstanding dwarven mechanics have observed dig-widgets and recognized the complexity of the technology, they've steadfastly refused to adapt something with such an unscrupulous origin.
</aside>
name: Dig-Widget

The cythnigot is a foul fungal parasite that grows and thrives in the corpses of small creatures. It wears these bodies like a suit, but also adjusts and tailors the fleshy covering to suit its needs, and the body ends up looking as alien as anything else spawned from the [Abyssal](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) depths. The cythnigot's most identifying feature is the long stalk of fungal material that extends from creature's body, ending in a surprisingly strong set of fanged jaws. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 17 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 [Cythnigot](/Monsters.aspx?ID=772) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 212 **Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); [telepathy (touch only)](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +6 **Will** +5 **HP** 14 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Resistances** mental 3, physical 3 (except cold iron) **Weaknesses** lawful 3 --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** bite +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d10+1 piercing plus 1d4 chaotic and tangle spores **Occult Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **1st** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) - **2nd** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; lawful only), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) - **4th** [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247) **Sickening Display** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The cythnigot presents its awful appearance fully, and creatures in a 10-foot emanation must attempt a DC 17 Will save. Once a creature attempts this save, it's temporarily immune to further Sickening Displays for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until its next turn.
**Failure** The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and is flat-footed for as long as it's sickened.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and is flat-footed for as long as it's sickened. **Tangle Spores** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) A creature bitten by a cythnigot becomes afflicted by fast-growing spores that swiftly grow into twitching spikes and hideous pallid growths of hairlike fibers. These growths erupt from the bite wound and writhe and wrap around the creature's limbs. [Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to save against tangle spores; **Saving Throw** DC 17 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 round); **Stage 2** clumsy 1 and [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) (1 round); **Stage 3** clumsy 2, flat-footed, and if you attempt a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action, you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or it's lost; roll the check after spending the action, but before any effects are applied (1 round).
name: Cythnigot
