Shoggti travel to other planes in roving bands to capture and charm victims, bringing them back to the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) where they are enslaved for unknown—but no doubt insidiously evil—purposes. These squid-like qlippoth have slick, blue skin and four suckered tentacles that end in dexterous pincers. In addition to a lamprey-like mouth at the base of its body, each has a secondary maw in the middle of its forehead that stretches from between its two bulging eyes to the back of its head. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Shoggti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=773) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 213 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +12 **Will** +14 **HP** 105 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Resistances** mental 5, physical 5 (except cold iron) **Weaknesses** lawful 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** bite +18 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+8 piercing plus 1d6 chaotic **Melee** pincer +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing plus 1d6 chaotic and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 23 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (×3) - **2nd** [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) **Braincloud** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Open](/Traits.aspx?ID=123)) The shoggti caresses a creature with a tentacle and supernaturally erodes the creature's capability for thought as it does so. The target must succeed at a DC 25 Will save or become [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), with an unlimited duration. The target can attempt the save to remove the stupefied condition again once per day when it makes its daily preparations. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d10+8 bludgeoning, DC 25 **Fascinating Display** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The shoggti writhes its tentacles and shifts its coloration to put on an unnaturally fascinating hypnotic display. Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a DC 25 Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Fascinating Displays for 1 minute.
Critical Success The creature is unaffected.
Success The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
Failure The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) for 1 minute. Once the fascination effect ends, the creature is sickened 1.
Critical Failure The creature is fascinated for 1 minute, can't use reactions while fascinated, and is sickened 2 once the fascination ends.

Little more than a coiled mass of intestines encircling a massive gaping maw, this qlippoth is an [Abyssal](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) scavenger, subsisting on the filth and leftovers of [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) and qlippoth alike, although it relishes any opportunity to consume living prey. Despite this seeming lowly role in the Abyssal ecosystem, a nyogoth is far from a stupid beast and can orchestrate cunning ambushes to secure its next meal. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Nyogoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=774) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 214 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); [telepathy 100 ft.](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +19 **Will** +16 **HP** 175 **Immunities** acid, [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Resistances** mental 10; physical 10 (except [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375)) **Weaknesses** lawful 10 Caustic Blood** **Trigger** The nyogoth takes piercing or slashing damage; **Effect** The nyogoth sprays its acidic blood on adjacent creatures, dealing 6d6 acid damage (DC 29 basic Reflex save). --- **Speed** 5 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** jaws +23 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+13 piercing plus 2d6 acid, 1d6 chaotic, and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tentacle mouth +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+13 piercing plus 1d6 acid, 1d6 chaotic, and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 26, attack +20 - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) (at will) - **4th** [Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2) (at will), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **5th** [Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42) **Feeding Frenzy** **Requirement** The nyogoth has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) a creature; **Effect** The nyogoth slavers and chews at the grabbed creature, dealing 2d6+7 slashing and 1d6 acid damage (DC 29 basic Fortitude save). **Nauseating Display** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The nyogoth untangles its tentacles and prolapses its many mouths, turning itself inside out in a truly nauseating display. Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a DC 29 Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Nauseating Displays for 1 minute.
**Critical** Success The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The creature is [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and sickened 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 5 and sickened 2.

The chernobue infects all creatures it encounters with itself, spreading pain and calamity wherever it flops and writhes. It sheds ruin and sups on anguish, but a chernobue can sometimes be persuaded to pause for a few moments of conversation if its partner in discourse can keep its attention by providing enough atrocious details. Resembling a tumor-like growth of oily blackness, this vile monstrosity has numerous muscular tentacles, a single glaring baleful eye, and a drooling, toothy maw in the middle of its body. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Chernobue](/Monsters.aspx?ID=775) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 214 **Perception** +25; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +22 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +18 **Will** +25 **HP** 220 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Resistances** mental 10, physical 10 (except cold iron) **Weaknesses** lawful 10 **Aura of Order's Ruin** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 30 feet. A lawful or good creature that begins its turn in this aura's emanation must attempt a DC 29 Will save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (lawful good creatures instead become sickened 2).
**Boiled by Light** A chernobue takes 2d10 points of fire damage each time it starts its turn in an area of bright light.
**Recall Venom** **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet suffers the effects from stage 2 of rupturing venom; **Effect** The chernobue calls out telepathically to the semi-alive toxin, causing it to burst out of the target's body and slither through the air to drain back into one of the chernobue's mouths. The poisoned creature takes 7d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 32 basic Fortitude save) as the venom exits its body, but is thereafter cured of rupturing venom, and the chernobue regains an equal number of Hit Points. --- **Speed** 30 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Melee** jaws +26 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d10+13 piercing plus 1d6 chaotic and rupturing venom **Melee** tentacle mouth +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+13 piercing plus 1d6 chaotic **Occult Innate Spells** DC 32 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will) - **5th** [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=314) - **6th** [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=218), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self only) - **Constant (4th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Paralyzing Display** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The chernobue's eye pulses and its lid peels back to reveal mind-bending awfulness. Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a DC 32 Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Paralyzing Displays for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1d4 rounds. It can attempt a new save to end the effect at the end of each of its turns.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but paralyzed for 1 minute. **Rupturing Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The thick, orange venom injected by a chernobue is semi-alive, and as it seethes in a creature's body, it deals poison damage in addition to bludgeoning damage as it ruptures flesh; **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 32; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison and 2d6 bludgeoning (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison, 2d6 bludgeoning, and [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round)
name: Chernobue

The brutish and gluttonous augnagar live to feast— preferably on rotten flesh, and when possible, demon flesh. But to them, the greatest delicacy is the flesh of other augnagars. Augnagars have spiderlike legs with leathery membranes like a bat's wings, and three tails ending in hooked claws perfect for slicing flesh. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Augnagar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=776) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 216 **Perception** +27; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +8 **Int** -2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +23 **Will** +25 **HP** 225 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Resistances** mental 15, physical 15 (except cold iron) **Weaknesses** lawful 15 --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** bite +28 ([Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus 4d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), 1d6 chaotic, and rotting curse **Melee** sting +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus 4d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 1d6 chaotic **Occult Innate Spells** DC 31 - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (×3) - **Constant (7th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Confusing Display** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The augnagar's writhing limbs and flesh seethe and squirm in a disorienting and unsettling manner. Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a DC 34 Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Confusing Displays for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 1 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature can't attempt a flat check to recover from confusion whenever it takes damage from an attack or spell. **Inhale Vitality** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The augnagar inhales sharply, drawing life force out of creatures in a 50-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 14d6 negative damage (DC 34 basic Fortitude save, and the creature is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) on a failure). The augnagar becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 round on its next turn, and it can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike. **Rotting Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 day); **Stage 2** drained 2 and the creature displays hideous, festering wounds exuding a horrific stench. Any creature within 30 feet of the victim must succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (plus [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) on a critical failure, for as long as it's sickened). While within the aura, affected creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute. The victim of the disease doesn't roll a save, but takes the failure effects automatically and can't reduce its sickened value below 1 (1 day).
Augnagars and Thulgants An augnagar that gorges sufficiently, especially on other augnagars, can grow so massive it can't even move, thrashing and festering where it lies. It eventually flies into a frenzy of self-cannibalism as it rips apart its own flesh to feast on. From the ruinous remains emerges a [thulgant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=777)—a smaller but more powerful qlippoth. Scholars are uncertain if other qlippoth are capable of transforming themselves in this fashion. So far, none have returned from the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) with proof.
name: Augnagar

Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a DC 40 Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Stunning Displays for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The creature is stunned 4.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 8. **Thulgant Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 40; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and the victim gains one of the following at random: [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 round); **Stage 2** 6d6 poison damage and the victim gains two of the following at random: clumsy 2, enfeebled 2, or stupefied 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 9d6 poison damage and the victim gains all three of the following: clumsy 3, enfeebled 3, and stupefied 3 (1 round)

markdown:
Few creatures can match a quickling's speed. These malicious fey creatures delight in striking with blinding speed and accuracy, racing in to stab and slash at foes before scampering away for cover with maddening— even frightening—swiftness. Stories of people simply doubling over, bleeding from what appear to be spontaneously opening wounds, are often eyewitness accounts of quickling attacks. Quicklings actively hate the “slow-paced dullards” they encounter every day, delighting in inflicting suffering and pain on creatures they deem too sluggish to matter. While they are related to kinder fey such as [brownies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=575) and [pixies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=391), quicklings care only for their own merriment. They see their own cruel pranks as the height of humor and have full confidence that their speed and invisibility will keep them safe from harm. They occasionally form gangs of up to a dozen individuals, although the group usually devolves very quickly into mayhem, as they inevitably perform increasingly deadly pranks against each other. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Quickling](/Monsters.aspx?ID=778) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 218 **Perception** +9; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +6, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +11 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Lethargy Poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=120) (3 doses), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +13 **Will** +8 **HP** 25 **Weaknesses** cold iron 5 **Slow Susceptibility** The quickling takes a –2 status penalty to saving throws against effects that cause the [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) condition. If the quickling ever becomes slowed, they lose their supernatural speed, can't Fade from View, and become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) for the duration of the slow. They also can't reduce this sickened condition for the duration of the slowed effect.
**Can't Catch Me** **Trigger** The quickling is targeted by a Strike; **Effect** The quickling darts aside, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC, then Strides up to half their Speed after the Strike resolves. --- **Speed** 100 feet **Melee** shortsword +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing plus lethargy poison **Primal Innate Spells** DC 20 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) - **1st** [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) - **2nd** [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=279) **Fade from View** **Requirements** The quickling used no [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions in the previous round; **Effect** The quickling becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) until it uses an attack, manipulate, or move action. The quickling can't use Fade from View again for 1d4 rounds. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The quickling's Strikes deal an extra 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Supernatural Speed** The quickling's speed, combined with nearly instantaneous acceleration and deceleration, enables them to move in astonishing ways. As long as they have a firm surface to travel across, they can Stride their full movement vertically or horizontally. They can even run across unstable surfaces, such as water, in the same way, although dangerous surfaces (acid, lava, etc.) harm them as normal. They must end their movement on a horizontal surface capable of bearing their weight or else they fall.
Quickling Pranks Quicklings consider cruelty to be the pinnacle of pranking. As a relatively minor example, a quickling may spend an entire day constantly moving food out of reach of a starving man. More commonly, however, a quickling prefers to cause pain and injury directly, such as by suddenly replacing a dinner fork with a red-hot knife as it's picked up, or slashing the legs of a traveler moving through tall grass.
name: Quickling

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Quoppopak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=779)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Quoppopaks combine the most terrifying aspects of water striders, octopuses, and vampire bats into one evil, water-dwelling monster. Their bodies consist of an oval abdomen, eight hollow tentacles, and a thick, tail-like limb ending in a ventral tube, which resembles a lamprey's mouth and is capable of intaking fluids at an astounding rate. While quoppopaks can clumsily maneuver on land, they prefer to move on water; quoppopaks glide along the surface of lakes or the ocean by thrusting their ventral tubes into the water, cycling liquid up through their bodies, then spraying the water back out in a high-pressure stream through their hollow, spear-like tentacles. Though the ventral tube can't expel fluids, it still poses a substantial threat to other creatures. Quoppopaks have gills, but they are located inside the creatures' abdomens, allowing them to draw their air from the water they siphon through their ventral tubes. Quoppopaks can hold some water in their abdomens for quick forays on land, but this is akin to humans holding their breath to go underwater, and the monsters' water reserves quickly deplete.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Quappokap.png" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>

<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +22; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +6

**Con** +5

**Int** -2

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31

**Ref** +23 

**Will** +18 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 195

</row>

**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** <actions string="Reaction" /> Tentacle only.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, swim 40 feet, water glide 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +24 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+13 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ventral tube +24 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d6+13 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
water jet +23 ([range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)),
**Damage** 2d6+13 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) 5 feet

**Flooding Thrust** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The quoppopak's ventral tube is in water; **Effect** The quoppopak uses Tentacle Stab against a creature within reach; if either of these Strikes hits, the creature must attempt a DC 30 Reflex save. On a failed save, the quoppopak shoots water through its tentacles into the creature, dealing 2d6+5 bludgeoning damage and making the creature [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) as its internal organs fill with water (double damage and sickened 2 on a critical failure).

**Tentacle Stab** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The quoppopak makes two tentacle Strikes against the same creature. These Strikes deal piercing damage instead of bludgeoning, and the quoppopak can't [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) with them. Its multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks.

**Water Glide**   The quoppopak can stand and move on the surface of water or other liquids without falling through. It can go underwater if it wishes, but it must [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) to do so.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Coastal Hunters</title>

Quoppopaks prefer to hunt at dawn and dusk, prowling seashores and estuaries for fresh meat. While they could easily live off of the minerals and tiny organisms they filter from the water sucked through their ventral tubes, they delight in killing living creatures. Despite their cruel natures, quoppopaks don't mind hunting alongside others of their kind if it means they'll be able to create even more carnage.
</aside>
name: Quoppopak

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Radiant Warden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=780)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The enigmatic and strange radiant wardens were constructed thousands of years ago to protect observatories and scholars against the incursion of alien aggressors from the Dominion of the Black. Over time, their roles as guardians expanded to include watching over any region where the laws of time and space have worn thin, particularly near portals and permanent gates between planets, planes, or dimensions.

 Named for both the radial nature of the concentric rings that make up their bodies as well as the glowing radiance of their attacks, radiant wardens carry on their orders, defending sites from invasion with single-minded purpose.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 38

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/RadiantWarden.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 17">[Radiant Warden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=780)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=35" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 220

**Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
any one ancient language (such as [Jistkan](/Languages.aspx?ID=59))

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +32, [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +36, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +32

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +9

**Dex** +6

**Con** +5

**Int** +6

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 40 

**Fort** +32 

**Ref** +29 

**Will** +28 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 300

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), nonlethal attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
mental 15, physical 15 (except adamantine)

**Gatekeeper Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 60 feet. A creature that uses a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) ability within the aura's emanation or enters it via a teleportation ability must succeed a DC 38 Will save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) and have its destination changed to a point of the radiant warden's choosing within the emanation. On a successful save, the creature arrives as intended but is still sickened 1.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hammer +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)),
**Damage** 3d12+15 bludgeoning plus radiant blow

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
radiant beam +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d10 force

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 38
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will)
- **7th**
[Dimensional Lock](/Spells.aspx?ID=71), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233)
- **8th**
[Collective Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=43) (×3), [Scintillating Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=267)
- **9th**
[Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338)

**Orrery** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) Until it acts, the radiant warden appears to be an orrery (or similar large mechanical contraption, such as a telescope). It has an automatic result of 53 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to convincingly pass as such a machine.

**Radiant Blast** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The radiant warden releases a 50-foot cone of bright energy that deals 10d12 force damage (DC 38 basic Reflex save). The radiant warden can't use Radiant Blast for 1d4 rounds.

**Radiant Blow**   When a creature is hit by the radiant warden's hammer Strike, a flash of radiant energy attempts to anchor the creature in place. The creature must attempt a DC 38 Will save; on a failure, the creature can't use any [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effects for 1 minute. On a critical failure, the creature is also permanently [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Ancient Intellects</title>

Each radiant warden's animating force consists of raw positive energy fused to the soul of a willing sacrifice—usually an astronomer or scholar near the end of their natural life. Focused now on protecting a site from any intrusion— including by curious archaeologists or adventurers—a radiant warden might pause before an attack if approached peacefully. Unfortunately, these constructs are prone to speaking in vague riddles or complex mathematical diatribes that can be as confusing as they are intriguing; often, discussions break down as frustration mounts on either side (or both).
</aside>
name: Radiant Warden

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Raven](/Monsters.aspx?ID=781)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These omnivorous birds are cunning and opportunistic scavengers. Capable of solving simple puzzles in order to retrieve desired items, ravens gather at the periphery of civilization, raiding it as needed when they are not hunting in the wilds. Whether singularly, in pairs, or as a flock, ravens live off scraps as often as they do carrion or prey. They are also unusually social among their own kind, even alerting other ravens to large carcasses with loud, croaking cries; it is rumored that this extends between flocks as well.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Cunning** A raven can use simple items as tools, such as poking a stick at an opening to tease out a piece of food. They are also quite adept at stealing objects. A raven can't use [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) to [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=67), [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69), or [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70), but it can use Thievery to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) light objects that it can carry in its beak or talons or to accomplish other relatively simple tasks.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Raven Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=782)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A flock of ravens is known as an unkindness. Certainly the name lives up to its meaning when a swarm of ravens decides to work together. In most cases, a raven swarm like the one presented here won't attack larger foes, but when manipulated by supernatural forces or simple desperation born from hunger, an unkindness of ravens can be a surprisingly dangerous foe.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Raven Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=782)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=9" />
<trait label="Swarm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=239" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 221

**Perception** +9; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** -4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30

</row>

**Immunities**
precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36)

**Resistances**
bludgeoning 2, piercing 5, slashing 5

**Weaknesses**
area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 40 feet

**Enraged Cunning**   There are few things as dangerously persistent in the natural world as an angry unkindness of ravens. A raven swarm can hound its prey through most barriers. Simple latches, unsecured chimney flues, loosely shuttered windows, and similar obstacles rarely keep an unkindness away. A raven swarm attempts a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to bypass many of these simple obstructions, typically against DC 20.

**Swarming Beaks** <actions string="Single Action" />  The ravens' angry pecking deals 1d8 piercing damage to each enemy in the swarm's space (DC 20 basic Reflex save). A creature that critically fails its save is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1d4 rounds as the ravens focus their attacks on the target's vulnerable face.

</column>

The ravener presented here was once an [ancient red dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=138). **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 47 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 47 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 [Ravener](/Monsters.aspx?ID=783) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 224 **Perception** +37; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33), smoke vision, soulsense 60 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Orcish](/Languages.aspx?ID=9) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +32, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +39, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +38, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +40, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair an ancient red ravener's vision; it ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +34 **Will** +37 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 500 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** cold 20, good 20 **Cowering Fear** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 42.
**Dragon Heat** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) 10 feet, 4d6 fire damage (DC 41 basic Reflex save)
**Soul Ward** 200 HP. An intangible field of necromantic energy protects a ravener from total destruction. A soul ward has 150 maximum Hit Points, or 200 if the ravener is level 21 or higher. Whenever a ravener would be reduced below 1 Hit Point, all damage in excess of what would reduce them to 1 Hit Point is instead dealt to their soul ward. If this damage reduces the soul ward to fewer than 0 Hit Points, the ravener is destroyed. A soul ward’s Hit Points can be restored only via specific ravener abilities such as Consume Soul, ravenous breath, or vicious criticals. A ravener who goes more than a week without successfully using Consume Soul to feed on a dying creature starves, and their soul ward loses 1d4 Hit Points each day until they feed. If the ravener’s soul ward loses all its Hit Points while the ravener still has more than 1 HP, they become a ravener husk.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Jaws only.
**Discorporate** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** The ravener takes excess damage to their soul ward but still has at least 51 Hit Points in their soul ward; **Effect** The ravener draws deeply into their soul ward, discorporating their body into soul energy in order to escape. They take 50 damage to their soul ward and their physical body vanishes, reappearing 1d4 hours later in a random location within 1 mile from the location where they used Discorporate.
**Redirect Fire** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11)) **Trigger** A creature within 100 feet casts a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) spell, or a fire spell otherwise comes into effect from a source within 100 feet; **Effect** The ravener makes all the choices to determine the targets, destination, and other effects of the spell, as though they were the caster. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+17 piercing plus 3d6 fire and 2d6 negative **Melee** claw +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+17 slashing plus 2d6 negative **Melee** tail +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+15 slashing plus 2d6 negative **Melee** wing +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+15 slashing plus 2d6 negative **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 44 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **4th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will) - **8th** [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) (at will) **Consume Soul** ([death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** A living creature within 30 feet of the ravener dies; **Effect** The ravener tears the creature’s soul from its body with their maw and gulps it down. The dying creature must attempt a DC 44 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The ravener tears off a small chunk of the creature’s soul. If the victim is restored to life, they are [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) in addition to any other side effects of returning to life. The ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to half the creature’s level.
**Failure** As success, but the creature’s soul is ravaged. The creature is drained 3 and the ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to the creature’s level.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the ravener devours the entire soul. The victim can’t be restored to life as long as the ravener exists except via a 10th-level effect such as [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196) or [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377), and the ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to twice the creature’s level. **Manipulate Flames** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The ravener attempts to take control of a magical [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) or a fire spell within 100 feet. If it succeeds at a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check (counteract level 10, counteract modifier +34), the original caster loses control of the spell or magical fire, control is transferred to the ravener, and this action counts as the ravener having Sustained the Spell with the action (if applicable). The ravener can choose to end the spell instead of taking control, if they choose. **Ravenous Breath Weapon** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The ravener breathes a blast of flame that deals 20d6 fire damage plus 4d6 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (DC 44 basic Reflex save). A creature that fails its save is also [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (or drained 2 on a critical failure). If a creature is drained by the ravener's Ravenous Breath Weapon, the ravener's soul ward gains 5 HP. The ravener can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Vicious Criticals** The ravener treats an attack roll as a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20, as long as the attack roll was a success. Additionally, whenever the ravener makes a critical hit with one of their Strikes, the target must succeed on a Fortitude save or gain the [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) condition. If the target already has a drained value of greater than 0, their drained value instead increases by 1, to a maximum of drained 4. Whenever the ravener applies drain to a creature in this way, their soul ward gains 5 Hit Points.
name: Ravener

Raveners require a steady diet of souls, and a ravener that's unable to feed for too long eventually cannibalizes their own soul. Should a ravener's soul ward ever be reduced to 0 Hit Points by hunger while the ravener has more than 1 Hit Point (see Soul Ward), they lose all traces of their former identity and descend into a feral, nearly mindless state. Even if a ravener husk later consumes soul energy, the transformation can be reversed only via Ravenous Repast. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 37 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Ravener Husk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=784) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 224 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), soulsense 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +0 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +22 **Will** +26 **HP** 325 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** good 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 31
**Boneshatter** **Trigger** The ravener husk takes any amount of bludgeoning damage; **Effect** The ravener's brittle bones shatter, spraying bone shards everywhere. Every creature within a 10-foot emanation of the ravener husk takes 7d6 piercing damage (DC 31 basic Reflex save). --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+16 piercing plus 2d6 negative **Melee** claw +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d4+16 slashing plus 2d6 negative **Breath Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) The ravener husk breathes a torrent of negative energy that deals 16d6 negative damage in a 40-foot cone (DC 34 basic Reflex save). They can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Ravenous Repast** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The ravener husk makes a jaws Strike against a deceased creature that has been dead no longer than 1 minute, was good aligned, and was at least level 15 in life. The ravener attempts a DC 5 flat check; if successful, they transform back into a ravener with 1 Hit Point in their soul ward.
name: Ravener Husk

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Stingray](/Monsters.aspx?ID=785)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Found in salt or fresh water, stingrays are normally passive creatures, but when threatened or cornered, they lash out with their tails to lance foes with their toxic stingers. Stingrays often bury themselves in mud, sand, or sea grass, and unfortunately for those wading in shallow waters, stepping on an unseen stingray is a surefire way to receive a painful sting.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Manta Ray](/Monsters.aspx?ID=786)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

While manta rays might appear daunting, they are generally docile. Manta rays are found in temperate or tropical oceans, and they generally stay in the upper reaches or at the surface of deeper waters. These rays are filter feeders that subsist on plankton. They can also eat larger fish or aquatic creatures as the need arises, but rarely attack anything Small or larger. Many underwater cultures, particularly aquatic elves, raise and farm manta rays to serve as messengers, beasts of burden, or even pets. Manta rays don't make particularly good mounts, but some aquatic civilizations use them to draw underwater carriages.

</column>

markdown:
Revenants are obsessed, undead stalkers who arise from their own murders and are driven by only one thing: revenge against their killers. The common wisdom is that revenants arise only from individuals who have been utterly betrayed or abandoned to die a grueling death, but even then such victims might not rise from their graves. In other cases, revenants might even rise from what might legitimately be considered an accident if the revenant doesn't understand the full circumstances of their demise. In such cases, it doesn't matter that the “murderer” may not have intended to kill, for revenants understands no pity and can never forgive. Revenants have little memory of their lives other than anything they might need to recall in order to achieve their goal of vengeance. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Revenant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=787) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 227 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), sense murderer **Languages** any one spoken in life by their murderer (typically [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Sense Murderer** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141)) A revenant knows the direction of their murderer (as long as both are on the same plane), but not the distance. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 115 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** physical 5 (except slashing) **Self-Loathing** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) If a revenant sees their own reflection or any object that was important to them in life, they must attempt a DC 25 Will save.
**Critical Success** The revenant is unaffected and can no longer be affected by that reflection or object in this way.
**Success** The revenant is distracted by self-loathing and becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Failure** The revenant becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the source that triggered their self-loathing and does everything they can to destroy it until the end of the revenant's next turn.
**Critical Failure** The revenant becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) as long as the source of their self-loathing is apparent, until they're attacked, or until they see their murderer.
**Undying Vendetta** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) If the revenant's murderer dies, the revenant is immediately destroyed. A revenant that can't sense their murderer must attempt a DC 11 flat check once every 24 hours to avoid becoming [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31); they immediately rise again once they can sense their murderer. A murderer who becomes undead does not trigger the revenant's destruction until the murderer is finally destroyed. The revenant gains a +2 status bonus to checks and DCs against their murderer. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+5 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Baleful Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The revenant wails horribly. Each creature within a 60-foot burst must attempt a DC 23 Will save. Regardless of the outcome of their saving throw, affected creatures are then immune to Baleful Shriek for 1 hour. The revenant's murderer never improves their degree of success due to this ability's incapacitation trait. The revenant can't use Baleful Shriek again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and paralyzed for 1d4 rounds. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d6+5 bludgeoning, DC 24
Exceptions to Evil While most undead are evil, revenants are not—these unusual stalkers rise not out of a sense of cruelty or hatred of the living, but spontaneously from the need for vengeance following a deep betrayal. One can avoid a revenant's wrath by simply getting out of its way—unless you happen to be its reason for unlife!
name: Revenant

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Rhinoceros](/Monsters.aspx?ID=788)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Rhinoceroses are short-tempered, territorial, and easily startled, and these traits combined with their innate ferocity means their natural instinct when disturbed is to attack. When intruders disturb or surprise rhinoceroses, the animals respond by charging directly at the interlopers and then lashing out with their mighty horns.

Even bulkier than their non-woolly cousins, these rhinoceroses have a shaggy pelt of long, thick fur and a huge crescent-shaped horn. Woolly rhinos inhabit areas of arid tundra and cold steppes, spending much of their day grazing for sustenance. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Woolly Rhinoceros](/Monsters.aspx?ID=789) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 228 **Perception** +11; [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +13 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +5 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +11 **Will** +15 +2 status to all saves vs. cold **HP** 100 **Cold Adaptation** The woolly rhinoceros treats [environmental cold effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as if they were one step less extreme.
**[Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16)** --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** horn +16 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+6 piercing **Melee** foot +16, **Damage** 2d8+6 bludgeoning **Rhinoceros Charge** The woolly rhinoceros Strides twice, then makes a horn Strike. As long as the woolly rhinoceros moved at least 20 feet, the Strike's damage increases to 3d12+6. A Medium or smaller creature struck by this attack must succeed at a DC 24 Reflex save or be automatically [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) back 5 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) by the force of the blow. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Medium or smaller, foot, DC 21
name: Woolly Rhinoceros

markdown:
These androgynous, river-dwelling fey delight in manipulating the emotions of those unfortunate enough to fall into their grasp, using humiliation to break their victims' wills. Rusalkas enjoy keeping their broken toys nearby, both for continuing entertainment and to aid in the rusalkas' defense, as the shame their captives feel often drives them to become obsessively loyal to these fey. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Rusalka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=790) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 229 **Perception** +22; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +21, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +25 **Will** +21 **HP** 230 **Resistances** fire 10 **Weaknesses** cold iron 15 **Blurred Form** A rusalka is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while underwater. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 50 feet; [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) **Melee** tresses +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+10 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 35 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) (at will) - **5th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will), [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) (at will) - **Constant (6th)** [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) **Beckoning Call** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The rusalka cries out a compelling invitation. Each non-fey creature within a 300-foot emanation must attempt a DC 27 Will save. The effect lasts for 1 round, but if the rusalka uses Beckoning Call again on subsequent rounds, the duration extends by 1 round for all affected creatures. Once a creature succeeds at any save against Beckoning Call, that creature is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) and must spend each of its actions to move closer to the rusalka, avoiding obvious dangers. If a beckoned creature is adjacent to the rusalka, it stays still and doesn't act. If attacked by the rusalka, the creature is freed from captivation at the end of the rusalka's turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but if attacked by the rusalka, the creature can attempt a new save only at the start of its next turn, rather than being freed at the end of the rusalka's turn. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+10 bludgeoning, DC 32 **Entangling Tresses** A rusalka can have up to eight creatures grabbed within their tresses at a time. **Flowing Hair** The rusalka attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against each grabbed creature's Fortitude DC. The rusalka moves each creature they succeed against up to 10 feet and each creature they critically succeed against up to 20 feet. This movement must all be within reach of its tresses. **Shameful Touch** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The rusalka touches a creature within 5 feet using their hand, stirring up memories of regret and shame. The target must attempt a DC 35 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The creature is sickened 1 and [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 1 and stunned 1, and it must use its first action on its next turn to Strike itself, automatically hitting.
Blue Week Those living in areas where rusalkas dwell know well to avoid the water during the week-long period in early Sarenith when the fey become particularly active, a time known in many regions as Blue Week. Most villages prohibit swimming and fishing during this time, though the prohibition is difficult to enforce, as it takes place during the height of good weather. Particularly superstitious folk take care to lock their doors both from within and without.
name: Rusalka

markdown:
While some creatures have been fused to create a new entity that's graceful and beautiful, the Sandpoint Devil is the opposite. It has the hooves and body of a powerful horse, but it walks on its hind legs in a perverse parody of a humanoid gait. Its equine face has been distorted and elongated with skeletal, yellowed fangs and milky eyes. The pox-spotted hide along the creature's back bears a draconic tail with dark, spiny plates and two torn, bat-like wings. The legendary Sandpoint Devil appears only on moonless nights when mists gather around the coast. Most encounters end with missing livestock or children, with little left behind other than oddly placed hoofprints that reek of brimstone. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 34 **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Sandpoint Devil](/Monsters.aspx?ID=791) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 230 **Perception** +16; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +14 **Will** +16 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 165 **Immunities** fire, [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Weaknesses** cold iron 5 Attack of Opportunity Hoof only. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** jaws +20, **Damage** 2d10+12 piercing **Melee** hoof +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d6+12 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 23, attack +15 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) - **1st** [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (at will) - **2nd** [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) - **3rd** [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) **Accursed Breath** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The Sandpoint Devil breathes a 30-foot cone of flame that deals 6d10 fire damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 26 Reflex save. The Sandpoint Devil can't use Accursed Breath for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) by the agonizing pain of its burns.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes full damage and is cursed. The victim's flesh appears charred and burned, and the pain of being burned alive never fully vanishes. The cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to all checks from the constant pain. Healing doesn't alter the appearance of the burns or lessen the penalty, but removing the curse does. **Devil's Howl** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The Sandpoint Devil unleashes a bloodcurdling howl that can be heard for miles. Each creature within a 100-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or become [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) as long as it remains frightened on a critical failure). Creatures that attempt this Will save are then immune to Devil's Howl for 24 hours. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Medium or smaller, hoof, DC 23
Other Devils For over a decade, the legend of the Sandpoint Devil has persisted in western Varisia. Most hold that it's the fantasy of simple folk and booze-filled bellies, but locals insist it exists. Other creatures akin to the Sandpoint Devil might exist elsewhere in the world, with slight variations on their innate spells and the nature of their Accursed Breath.
name: Sandpoint Devil

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=792)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An immense, gnarled, tree awoken with raw, primal power by one of the fey Eldest of the First World, this monster—one of the legendary Tane—skitters on huge, spidery roots and thrashes its branches as fiery lightning courses within its blackened bark, a living manifestation of the violent clash between ancient forest and stormy sky.

<column gap="tiny">

**Splintering Death** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) When the sard dies, its body explodes in a 30-foot emanation. All creatures in the area take 10d6 electricity damage and 10d6 piercing damage (DC 43 basic Reflex save). Any creature that takes piercing damage is also exposed to sard venom.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
trunk +37 ([fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d6+18 bludgeoning plus 3d6 persistent electricity

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
branch +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d8+18 piercing plus sard venom

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
root +37 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d6+18 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
thorn +35 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range 180 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 4d4+16 piercing plus sard venom

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 41
- **7th**
[Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33) (×3)
- **8th**
[Punishing Winds](/Spells.aspx?ID=240), [Tree Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=343)

**Lightning-Struck Curse** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** The sard is about to damage a creature that has electricity resistance; **Effect** An instant before the target takes the electricity damage from the triggering event, the sard's electrical sparks glow red. The target must succeed at a DC 41 Will save or lose any electricity resistance it has until this curse is lifted.

**Sard Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162)) **Saving Throw** DC 41 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 10 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 electricity damage, 2d6 poison damage, and [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 electricity damage, 3d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 electricity damage, 4d6 poison damage, clumsy 2, and slowed 2 (1 round)

**Thorn Volley** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The sard makes up to four thorn Strikes, each against a different target. The sard's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks have been made.

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Huge or smaller, root, DC 39

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Witch-Tree</title>

The first sard was created when the Eldest known as [the Green Mother](/Deities.aspx?ID=93) beckoned the sky's rage to strike the eldest oak in her domain, transforming the tree into a living siege engine. This original sard still stands sentinel in the Hanging Bower of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10).
</aside>
name: Sard

A ramshackle collection of materials in a human shape, the scarecrow construct is indistinguishable from a normal scarecrow until it slowly creaks to life. As it animates, its carved pumpkin or sackcloth face bursts into eldritch flame, sending fear creeping into the air around it. Each scarecrow is handcrafted and unique in its appearance, though most are 5 to 6 feet tall and constructed of a combination of wood, cloth, rope, straw, sawdust, discarded husks and cobs, and similar materials, all dressed in ragged pastoral garments. This rudimentary construction makes a scarecrow somewhat fragile, prone to snapping limbs in the crush of battle. Yet its structure is adaptable, allowing it to reshape another piece of itself into a clawed limb or grip a severed portion of itself to swat at its foes. When a scarecrow is created, it must be anointed with a drop of its creator's blood into each of its eyes. This blood soaks into the material and siphons a tiny sliver of the creator's soul away—not enough to harm the creator, but more than enough to imbue the scarecrow with an instinctive intellect that allows it to follow commands as eagerly as a well-trained (if ill-tempered) guard dog. When a scarecrow is destroyed, the blood leaks back out from its eyes, but the portion of the creator's soul never returns. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Scarecrow](/Monsters.aspx?ID=793) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 232 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +8 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), nonlethal attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 5 (except slashing) **Weaknesses** fire 5 **Scarecrow's Leer** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 40 feet. The scarecrow's eyes flicker with an unnerving glow. A creature can't reduce its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition below 1 as long as it is in the aura's emanation.
When a creature enters or starts its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 18 Will save. Birds and other avian creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to this save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is frightened 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the scarecrow until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but frightened 3. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** claw +13 ([versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning plus clawing fear **Baleful Glow** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The scarecrow's head bursts into ghostly, heatless flame that sheds bright light in a 20-foot emanation (and dim light to the next 20 feet). If the scarecrow uses this ability on the first round of combat, any creature that has not acted yet is startled and becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the scarecrow for 1 round. It can suppress the light by using this action again. **Clawing Fear** The scarecrow's strikes deal an additional 1d6 mental damage to [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) creatures. **Mundane Appearance** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) Until it acts, the scarecrow resembles an ordinary scarecrow. It has an automatic result of 32 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to pass as an ordinary scarecrow.
Odds and Ends Scarecrows hold a certain liminal space that is inhabitable by spirit creatures. [Ghosts](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51) in particular can use their malevolent possession on a scarecrow as if it were a living creature, using them as vehicles to escape their site-bound nature and so further spread their murder and mayhem.
name: Scarecrow

Strange creatures born from jagged crystals in the heart of the [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2), sceaduinar are fueled by its negative energy and driven to extinguish all life. Resembling crystalline gargoyles with serrated limbs and sharp, bat-like faces, sceaduinar stand about 7 feet tall and weigh around 100 pounds. These malevolent beings sometimes gather in great packs of their own kind. Despite their intelligence, they act like cunning, feral beasts for the most part, though they occasionally build tools to aid them in extinguishing life. Sceaduinar sail through the great voids of their home plane, seeking to destroy any sparks of life that find their way into that deadly realm—even the twisted sparks found in undead creatures. When they discover portals to other planes, sceaduinar swarm through in great numbers, slaughtering all they come across. While dwelling outside the Negative Energy plane is uncomfortable for sceaduinar, they can exist for extended periods of time apart from their home. Of course, the feeling of a plane where negative energy isn't the rule doesn't improve these creatures' attitude, and as a result, they tend to be particularly cruel and violent when encountered in such realms. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Sceaduinar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=794) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 233 **Perception** +15; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), lifesense 120 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +18 **Will** +14 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 100 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) **Resistances** physical 5 (except adamantine) **Weaknesses** good 10 **Void Child** Sceaduinars have neither souls nor the ability to create. A sceaduinar is immune to effects that target a soul (such as [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21) or [resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20)) or that require knowledge of a creature's identity (such as [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141)), and critically fails [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks.
**Wing Flash** **Trigger** A creature attempts a melee attack against a sceaduinar or an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29) the sceaduinar's space; **Effect** The sceaduinar flexes its wings to emit a brief pulse of negative energy that deals 4d6 negative damage to the triggering creature (DC 22 basic Reflex save). --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** jaws +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+4 piercing plus 2d6 negative and drain life **Melee** wing +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+4 slashing plus 2d6 negative **Occult Innate Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35) - **2nd** [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) - **3rd** [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×3) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) **Drain Life** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When the sceaduinar damages a living creature with its jaws Strike, the sceaduinar gains 5 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt to the creature by the sceaduinar increases the drained value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4. **Entropic Touch** Negative damage dealt by a sceaduinar damages [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and creatures with negative healing as if it were positive damage. The sceaduinar's melee Strikes have the benefits of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune on attacks against [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) undead.
Sceaduinar Crystals Sceaduinar congregate on the great crystalline knots of negative energy that accrete in the [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2). Sceaduinar prod treelike crystal growths to produce dangerous items akin to spheres of annihilation or great structures that echo with eerie chimes. Sceaduinar also stimulate these aggregations of crystals to yield other sceaduinar and similar hateful creatures of unlife.
name: Sceaduinar

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Cave Scorpion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=795)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cave scorpions prefer to reside in underground lairs. Most have lost nearly all of their pigmentation, making them appear ghostly when they lurk at the edge of the light. While they prefer to eat large insects, cave scorpions can and will attack humanoids. Some Darklands societies have developed techniques to herd or lure scorpions into pest-infested tunnels and warrens at the edge of their settlements, where the scorpions thrive in a dual role of exterminators and guardians.
